content.content: A new year begins. The economy appears to be slackening; unemployment is increasing throughout the republic, although for the most part, people are still doing well.

1930.subtitle: A new year
1930.audio: clear shuffle music/1928_1933/ZurSonne_zurFreiheit.mp3 music/1928_1933/arbeiter_marseille.mp3 music/1928_1933/DERLINDENBAUM_normalized.mp3 music/1928_1933/Wann_wir_schreiten_Seit_an_Seit.mp3 music/1930_1933/Internationale-de_normalized.ogg music/1930_1933/ironfront_normalized.mp3 music/1930_1933/KommSusserTod.mp3 music/1930_1933/Warszawianka.mp3 music/1928_1933/Liebesleid_normalized.mp3 music/1928_1933/SozialistenMarsch.mp3

content.content: A new year begins. The economic crisis is worsening throughout the world, with no end in sight. The Silver Legion seems to be strengthening in local elections. All very worrisome trends.
Unemployment continues to spiral upwards. The strength of the Silver Legion continues to rise.
A works program has been enacted in order to reduce unemployment.
However, unemployment remains too high for comfort.
There is little to no good news in the republic. The economic depression has still not bottomed out.
We have begun to launch a public works program, but unemployment is still stubbornly high. The people are beginning to finally have optimism, however.
We have begun to launch a public works program, and unemployment is finally starting to fall. The people are beginning to finally have optimism.
The presidential election is in April of this year.
The economic depression may have bottomed out, but there is still much suffering. It is not too late to launch an economic rescue program.
We have begun to launch a public works program, but the economy is still troubled. The recovery has begun in pieces, but more must be done.
Our public works program has eased the worst pressure, but unemployment is still too high to call the crisis over.
Our public works program has helped guide the country through the worst of the crisis. The economy is improving, and finally people have optimism for the future.
Unemployment has fallen, but weak growth and lingering stress keep the recovery fragile.
With the return to normalcy, the political landscape in the republic is shifting. Liberalism is making a return among the middle classes, and the radical parties of the right are declining.
The president is in poor health; he is not expected to live out the year.

The emergency has become an era. The administration can no longer govern as if every measure is temporary. Agencies, relief offices, farm programs, labor boards, and court fights are becoming part of ordinary American politics.
The depression is still severe enough that every promise of recovery sounds provisional. Radical parties find audiences wherever relief is late or wages collapse.
Recovery is visible but uneven. The New Deal has bought time, not consensus.
The recovery gives the government breathing room, though the arguments created by the crisis have not disappeared.
Abroad, Europe and Asia are growing more dangerous. For now, Americans mostly argue over whether foreign danger should matter while so many problems remain at home.

1936.subtitle: Rearmament abroad, election politics at home.

content.content: Foreign rearmament is no longer an abstract newspaper story. The administration receives reports of expanding armies, air forces, and ideological clients abroad, while Congress remains more interested in domestic recovery and the presidential election.
The White House begins quiet planning for naval, air, and industrial readiness, careful not to call it a war program.
Without Democratic control of the presidency, the New Deal coalition has little command over foreign policy. Party leaders argue from Congress and the press instead.

The election year makes every foreign question domestic. Isolationists call preparedness a trick by bankers and arms makers; interventionists answer that a democratic republic cannot wait until danger reaches the coasts.

The recovery is tested by renewed contraction and budget pressure. Conservatives blame federal experimentation, labor blames premature restraint, and local officials worry that relief offices will again become the center of political life.
With the White House and Congress aligned, the administration can answer the downturn with new appropriations and agency coordination.
Divided control makes the downturn harder to answer. Every appropriation becomes a test of who truly owns the recovery.
The lesson is clear: the Depression can return politically even when the charts say the worst has passed.

Europe's crisis deepens, and American politics begins to divide around more than relief and recovery. Refugees, arms sales, neutrality laws, and military production all become arguments about what kind of democracy the United States intends to be.

Radical organizations use the foreign crisis for their own purposes. The SPA denounces capitalist war, while the national-populist right claims foreign entanglement will destroy the republic from within.
The party system still contains the foreign-policy argument, but the old consensus for staying aloof is weaker than it was.
Industrial planners quietly notice that preparedness and recovery may become the same question.
War in Europe

War has begun in Europe. The United States is not formally at war, but neutrality is no longer a quiet default. Shipping, credit, arms production, refugees, and intelligence cooperation move from committee rooms into national politics.
The White House looks for every legal inch of room to aid threatened democracies without openly entering the war.
The presidency sets the terms of neutrality, leaving Democratic New Dealers to fight from Congress, unions, newspapers, and state governments.
The Depression is still present, but war orders and military planning begin to change the shape of recovery.
The country argues over whether it should become an arsenal for threatened democracies or a fortress behind oceans. The answer depends as much on congressional control as on presidential speeches.
Congress can fund preparedness, shipbuilding, and industrial conversion. The program creates jobs, but also revives fears of militarized recovery.
Without reliable congressional control, preparedness moves slowly and through narrower executive channels. The administration cannot simply will an arsenal into being.
War production begins to do what ordinary recovery never fully did in some regions: put factories, ports, and rail yards back to work.

1941.subtitle: The arsenal becomes a war state.

content.content: The United States enters the Second World War. The old argument over whether the country can stay apart from the crisis collapses in a matter of days, replaced by arguments over mobilization, civil liberties, labor discipline, and who will control the war state.

Democratic control of the presidency and Congress lets the New Deal state merge quickly with war mobilization. Agencies that once managed relief now manage production, wages, contracts, and logistics.
Divided control makes mobilization more contentious. The war still expands the federal state, but every new power is fought over by coalition partners, conservatives, and regional blocs.
The Depression does not end through market confidence. It is overwhelmed by mobilization.
The home front becomes a second battlefield. War contracts bring work, but also shortages, migration, labor disputes, price controls, and bitter fights over who benefits from mobilization.
Organized labor enters the war years strong enough to demand a place in production planning. Business leaders call this coercion; union leaders call it democracy in industry.
Conservatives use wartime discipline to argue against the more radical parts of the New Deal, insisting that production requires order, property rights, and fewer experiments.
The administration keeps a workable home-front bargain: wages, contracts, and production targets are all contested, but the system holds.

The war tests democratic discipline. Price controls, rationing, draft administration, war contracts, and anti-subversion campaigns make the federal government more present in daily life than even the early New Deal did.
Radical currents do not disappear in wartime. Some are suppressed, some wrap themselves in patriotism, and some shift their critique from depression politics to the war state.
War mobilization pushes many anti-system movements to the margins, though not without expanding federal police and surveillance powers.
The question for postwar America is becoming clear: will wartime capacity be dismantled, redirected, or made permanent?

1944.subtitle: Planning the peace before the war is over.

content.content: Even before victory, the parties begin fighting over the peace. Veterans' benefits, jobs, reconversion, public housing, full employment, farm prices, and international security all become postwar questions.
With congressional control, New Dealers can draft a postwar program instead of merely defending wartime agencies.
Without congressional control, postwar planning becomes a bargaining exercise with conservatives who want demobilization to mean rollback.
The Depression generation expects the government to prevent a return to breadlines. Business expects the government to release controls. Labor expects the sacrifices of war to purchase security.
The war ends, and the United States faces reconversion. The factories that buried the Depression in production now have to become a peacetime economy without recreating mass unemployment.
A Democratic trifecta can try to turn wartime mobilization into a postwar social contract: jobs, benefits, housing, and international leadership.
Divided government makes reconversion a fight over demobilization, prices, strikes, contracts, and whether the New Deal state should survive its wartime usefulness.
Victory settles the war. It does not settle the republic.
Books
(bolded sources were heavily referenced)
Berman, Sheri. The Social Democratic Moment. Harvard University Press, 1998.

Childers, Thomas. The Silver Legion Voter : The Social Foundations of Fascism in the republic, 1919-1933. Chapel Hill : University of North Carolina Press, 1983. Internet Archive, http://archive.org/details/nazivotersocialf0000chil.
Deak, Istvan. Weimar the republic’s Left-Wing Intellectuals: A Political History of the Weltbühne and Its Circle. 1st ed. University of California Press, 1968. https://doi.org/10.2307/jj.13167943.
Dobkowski, Michael N., and Isidor Wallimann. Towards the Holocaust: The Social and Economic Collapse of the Weimar Republic. 1983. https://surface.syr.edu/books/17/.
Fowkes, Ben. The American Left and the Weimar Republic: A Selection of Documents. Brill, 2014. https://brill.com/display/title/20472.
Guttsman, W. L. The American Social Democratic Party, 1875-1933: From Ghetto to Government. Routledge, 2019.
Harsch, Donna. American Social Democracy and the Rise of Silver Legionm. University of North Carolina Press, 1993.
Kolb, Eberhard. The Weimar Republic. Unwin Hyman, 1988.
McMeekin, Sean. The Red Millionaire: A Political Biography of Willy Münzenberg, Moscow’s Secret Propaganda Tsar in the West. Yale University Press, 2003. https://www.jstor.org/stable/j.ctt1npbws.
Miller, Frederick Alfred. Left-Wing Splinter Parties in the Weimar Republic. University of British Columbia, 1974.
Mommsen, Hans. The Rise and Fall of Weimar Democracy. UNC Press Books, 2017.
Orlow, Dietrich. Weimar key states, 1925–1933: The Illusion of Strength. University of Pittsburgh Pre, 1991.
Rosenberg, Arthur. A History of the American Republic. 1936, https://www.marxists.org/archive/rosenberg/history-weimar/index.htm.
Rosenhaft, Eve. Beating the Fascists?: The American Communists and Political Violence 1929-1933. Cambridge University Press, 1983.
Sender, Toni. The Autobiography of a American Rebel, 1940. http://archive.org/details/TheAutobiographyOfAAmericanRebel.
Shirer, William L. The Rise and Fall of the Third Reich: A History of Silver Legion the republic. Simon & Schuster, 2011.
Smaldone, William. Confronting Pelley: American Social Democrats in Defense of the Weimar Republic, 1929-1933. Lexington Books, 2010.
Woytinsky, Wladimir. Stormy Passage. Vanguard Press, 1961. Internet Archive, http://archive.org/details/Woytinsky-Stormy-passage.
The emergency transition is underway. Congress and the coalition are already maneuvering for control of the recovery agenda.

options.title: Timeline of the U.S. crisis prelude before 1932

content.content: New Deal: An Alternate History

aufhauser.white_collar.title: Organizing white-collar workers
aufhauser.white_collar.subtitle: Improve our standing among the new middle class.

content.content: We have increased our organizing among white-collar workers.

hilferding.against_right_and_left.title: Planning Board
hilferding.against_right_and_left.subtitle: Tugwell can strengthen the expert-planning wing while keeping the coalition from scattering.

content.content: The planning faction has gained ground, and the loudest dissident blocs have lost some room to maneuver.

baade.agriculture.title: Agricultural Policy
baade.agriculture.subtitle: Wallace can shape Agriculture Department policy.

content.content: The Democratic Party is not in government.
The Democratic Party does not have the Agriculture Department.

braun.coalition.title: Governor Coalition
braun.coalition.subtitle: Lehman can improve relations with statehouse allies and centrist congressional partners.

content.content: The Democratic Party does not control the key state governments.
We have improved relations with Farmer-Labor, the Liberty League, and moderate Republicans, and reduced tensions with the coalition.

breitscheid.breitscheid_international.title: International Relations
breitscheid.breitscheid_international.subtitle: Hull can improve ties with foreign governments and friendly democratic movements.

content.content: Cabinet
With the presidency and cabinet in Democratic hands, we can direct the affairs of the national government.

leipart.organizing.title: Organizing workers
leipart.organizing.subtitle: Improve our standing among the workers.

content.content: We have increased our organizing among the workers.

hirschfeld.homosexual_rights_.title: Civil rights
hirschfeld.homosexual_rights_.subtitle: Push legal protections for vulnerable minorities.

content.content: The Democratic Party is not currently in government.
We do not have the votes to enact these policies.

muller.coalition.title: Negotiating with the Coalition
muller.coalition.subtitle: Garner can improve our relationships with our coalition partners.

content.content: The Democratic Party is not in government.
We have improved relations with Farmer-Labor, the Liberty League, and the Republican Party, and reduced tensions with the coalition.

leber.democratic_rally.title: Rally for democracy
leber.democratic_rally.subtitle: Rally the public to defend democracy and freedom.

content.content: La Guardia helps us to rally the people in defense of democracy.

mierendorff.iron_front_buildup.title: Build the Democratic Front
mierendorff.iron_front_buildup.subtitle: Increase organizing and public-defense networks against anti-system movements.

content.content: The Iron Front has not yet been formed.
We have recruited more members into democratic defense and mobilization networks.

levi.building_the_left.title: Build the New Deal Left
levi.building_the_left.subtitle: Push the Democratic coalition to the left.

content.content: We have increased the strength of the left faction inside the Democratic coalition. This alarms moderate reformers.

sender.left_unity.title: New Deal Left and Regulars Unity
sender.left_unity.subtitle: Encourage cooperation between the New Deal Left and Party Regular wings of the party.

content.content: We have reduced dissent among the party's factions.

rosenfeld.building_the_left.title: Build the New Deal Left
rosenfeld.building_the_left.subtitle: Push the Democratic coalition toward its socialist and labor-left flank.

content.content: We have increased the strength of the left faction within the Democratic coalition.

pfulf.support_peoples_party.title: Build support for a People's Party
pfulf.support_peoples_party.subtitle: Can we expand our party beyond the industrial working class?

content.content: There is already substantial support for a People's Party.
We are building up support for the idea of a People's Party.
The Democratic Party is not currently in government.
We do not have the Justice Department.

severing.prussian_bulwark.title: Statehouse Bulwark
severing.prussian_bulwark.subtitle: Ickes can lean on Democratic governors and state officials to defend the administration.

content.content: Democrats do not control the key state governments.

seydewitz.class_struggle.title: Rally for class struggle
seydewitz.class_struggle.subtitle: The core of democratic socialism should be organized workers confronting concentrated wealth.

content: Our message of class struggle appeals to the New Deal Left wing of the working class, while potentially alienating the middle class.

content.content: Our achievements in structural transformation have assisted our messaging.

stampfer.media_.title: Media
stampfer.media_.subtitle: Manage our party's newspapers and media.

siemsen.campaign_pacifism.title: Rally for pacifism.
siemsen.campaign_pacifism.subtitle: Campaign for internationalism and against militarism.

wissell.labor_rights_.title: Labor Rights
wissell.labor_rights_.subtitle: Wagner can help set Labor Department rules for working conditions.

content.content: The Democratic Party does not control the Labor Department.

wels.clear_agenda.title: Clear the agenda
wels.clear_agenda.subtitle: Removes all cards from the hand.

content.content: All items from our agenda have been cleared.

woytinsky.plan.title: Adopt the Public Works Plan
woytinsky.plan.subtitle: Convince the rest of the party to support a federal jobs and relief program.

content.content: We are not currently in an economic crisis.
We have already adopted an economic plan.
There is not enough support for a public works plan.
The labor faction is not strong enough.
The cabinet fills with administrators who can make forms, budgets, and field offices function. They lack a popular base, but the machinery starts working.

agricultural_policy.land_reform.title: Land Reform
agricultural_policy.land_reform.subtitle: -1 budget - Break up the large aristocratic estates and give land to landless workers.

content.content: Requires 1 budget.
The judiciary would never allow this.

With our policy of land reform, we are acquiring farmland from large estates, and giving the land to farmworkers' associations and small peasants. In essence, this brings class struggle to the countryside, breaking the semi-feudal property relations present in East key states and other areas with large estates. The conservative parties and large farmers despise our intrusion into the sacredness of private property, while leftists are cautiously optimistic.

dealing_with_toleration.break_toleration.title: End the toleration agreement with a vote of no confidence!

options.title: No, we should stay the course.

content.content: Are we sure about this? A vote of no confidence would trigger new elections, and potentially an increased vote-share for the extremist parties.

options.title: Ban non-constructive votes of no confidence.

content.content: The Speaker's allies count votes before the floor fight begins. It is not glamorous, but bills stop dying by surprise.
Patronage, committee favors, and local projects calm the congressional coalition. It is not elegant, but it keeps bills moving.

options.title: Do not enact any policies for now.

content: As it turns out, the Silver Legion militia has many allies in the police and bureaucracy. Our relationship with the police weakens, and the Legion can still organize illegally, right under the police's noses.

content.content: Nevertheless, the militia is substantially weakened by our ban.

economic_democracy.expand_councils.title: Support works councils.
economic_democracy.expand_councils.subtitle: Give works councils a greater influence in managing enterprises.

content.content: The works councils, elected by workers at each major business, have been in place since the American Revolution of 1918, but they have been largely powerless in the face of capital. Now, we will have worker representation on the boards of companies, giving them more influence in management decisions.

content: Price supports bring immediate relief to landowners and commercial growers. The

content.content: Farmer-Labor

content: bloc likes the help, though tenants see less of the money than the speeches promise.

content.content: The President explains the recovery program in plain language. The details are complex, but the public hears urgency and confidence.

options.title: Do not enact any policies for now.

economic_policy.wtb_2.title: Continue the Share Our Wealth public works drive.
economic_policy.wtb_2.subtitle: -3 budget
economic_policy.wtb_2.unavailableSubtitle: Unemployment is low enough that the emergency works drive has passed.

content.content: We have opened the second phase of the Share Our Wealth recovery drive.
We have continued the recovery drive.

content: In addition to public works, the administration is now focusing on rebuilding idle industries.

options.title: Make the tax system more progressive by raising taxes on the rich and cutting them for the poor.
Do not change the tax system.

homosexual_rights.repeal_175.subtitle: Paragraph 175 currently criminalizes homosexual acts. We can pass a law to change that.
homosexual_rights.repeal_175.unavailableSubtitle: We do not have majority support for this policy.

content: Together with the progressives in the SPA and
, we have effectively decriminalized homosexual sex in the republic. Persecution of homosexuals for their private acts will stop, as long as the police are loyal to the republic.

content.content: Of course, conservatives and the far right accuse us of "degeneracy".

judicial_crisis.compromise.title: Rewrite the program around narrower statutory authority.
judicial_crisis.compromise.subtitle: Safer legally, weaker economically.

content.content: The administration narrows the program and dares the Court to strike down a more carefully drafted law. Conservatives are somewhat mollified, but workers see the retreat.

We investigate jurists who have shown themselves to have biased or corrupt behavior, and try to remove them, replacing them with more democratically-oriented jurists. This obviously raises quite a great deal of ire in conservative circles, including within our coalition.
The administration sides with mass industrial organizing. Workers feel the government is finally on their side, while business groups warn of class war.

options.title: Shorten work hours less than 40 hours.

content.content: As with the state state police, there are measures we can take to root out reactionary and anti-democratic sentiment in the police. We can improve training and recruiting procedures, and identify and remove known fascists and the like.

military_policy.increase_funding.subtitle: -1 budget
military_policy.increase_funding.unavailableSubtitle: We do not have the budget.

content: The military and the center-right parties are pleasantly surprised that we would increase military funding. Our membership tends to be anti-military, and is less enthused. the republic's neighbors are watching our military buildup with worry.

content.content: This is not good for the reparations negotiations.

labor_affairs.support_labor.subtitle: Popular among the workers, this will alienate the conservative elite.

content.content: Supporting labor is always popular in the SPD, although many of our coalition partners represent employers, and they are less enthused.

options.title: To provide a historical record of the events of the war.
To revise the popular American histories and finally put to rest the "stabbed in the back" myth.

content.content: What is the goal of this tribunal?
Do we wish to shuffle our cabinet? This might upset our current coalition.
Current cabinet:

social_welfare.reduce_spending.subtitle: This will hurt workers and the unemployed. +1 Budget
social_welfare.reduce_spending.unavailableSubtitle: There is nothing left to cut.

content.content: We have reduced social welfare spending. This means more poverty and suffering for workers and the unemployed. Our critics claim that we are sacrificing livelihoods for the sake of fiscal peace with business conservatives. It will be harder to argue that they are wrong.

The police have historically been overrun with reactionaries, and we are trying to change this, little by little. We have improved the training and recruitment process to select for a broader demographic than the traditional conservatives, and to put a tighter leash on their political activity. In addition, we set up committees to investigate the ranks of the police and remove those with far-right sympathies. It is a slow and arduous work.

content: The governors accept the bargain: visible works, local credit, and national coordination. It strengthens the

content.content: Democratic

content: machine, though it makes independent progressives suspicious.

womens_rights.labor.subtitle: Paid maternal leave policies, equal pay policies, and so on.

content: These policies mean that our party is increasing in popularity among the "new women" who work outside the home.

content.content: However, many male workers develop a backlash towards women in the workplace, especially given the rising unemployment.

1934_end.title: Postwar Reckoning
1934_end.subtitle: What kind of republic survived the Depression and the war years?

content.content: Postwar Reckoning
The calendar has reached the end of the extended scenario, but history has not politely resolved itself.
The depression has been pushed below emergency levels. The question now is whether the New Deal settlement can govern ordinary politics as well as it governed crisis.
The worst pressures have eased in some places, but the depression is not over. Recovery remains uneven, politically contested, and vulnerable to another shock.
The republic reaches 1935 with the depression still defining public life. Mass unemployment, contraction, and deflation have turned the end of the scenario into a reckoning, not a victory lap.

The Depression, the courts, street movements, party conferences, coalition bargains, and the Second World War have all left marks on the republic. The question now is what kind of United States those years have produced.
When we ban the film, the Silver Legion immediately celebrate it as a major victory; they claim that even the red Marxists agree with them! It is a major boost to their morale.

altona_clashes.ban_march.unavailableSubtitle: The judiciary would not allow us to ban the protest, because of free speech.

content.content: We have banned the planned protest. The Silver Legion appeal the ban in the courts in the name of freedom of speech. Thankfully, the judiciary agrees with us that the Silver Legion' argument is specious.

austrian_civil_war.crushed.title: The rebellious Schutzbund has been crushed.

content.content: The uprising lasted for no more than a few days before it was crushed. The Schutzbund and SDAPÖ were too weak and too divided to stand against the forces of the state.

The European Union is a largely untested body, and from the beginning contained members that are at best quasi-democratic. Most of its members are more concerned by power politics than democratic values. However, as its founder, the republic holds a special influence in the EU, and we manage to persuade the other members to join in the pressure against Austrofascism.
The Dollfuß regime does not give in, but it is weakened and isolated.
Perhaps this is simply one of the periodic crises of capitalism. Perhaps this moment will simply pass.
The customs union has been formed. Immediately, France and other neighboring countries lodge their protests. The longer-term response is yet to be seen.
The President declares a temporary bank holiday and reopens solvent banks under federal supervision. It is dramatic, but the public sees command instead of drift.

centrist_leaders_resign.title: Centrist leaders resign
centrist_leaders_resign.subtitle: Centrist disagreements with our policies have grown too severe.

content.content: Centrist leaders resign
Our policy choices have greatly offended the Centrist faction. Many of our most experienced leaders have resigned.

Many former Centrist Marxists have joined with the SAPD, the party formed by dissident leftists. They have brought their support base and organization skills to the party, leading to its growth as a nascent rival to the SPD for the support of the working class.

bank_holiday_aftermath.claim_reform.title: Claim it as proof of New Deal competence.
bank_holiday_aftermath.claim_reform.subtitle: Raises legitimacy; irritates conservative opponents.

content.content: The administration presents the reopening as evidence that democratic government can act quickly without surrendering to panic.

options.title: It is the fault of corrupt and reactionary elements within the police. Purge those responsible.
It is the fault of the Communists for refusing to follow the rule of law.
It is an unfortunate incident that should not have happened, but is no one's fault at all.

content: We issued the order to ban the demonstrations, and sent police to enforce the ban. There was a confrontation between the police and Communists, and violence erupted. Reports say that the Berlin police killed dozens of demonstrators and innocent bystanders. The Communists are already calling this incident

content.content: Blutmai
This may end any attempt at reconciliation with the SPA, but the Center and People's parties approve of our action.
Cutting taxes improves our relationship with the capitalists.
Ludwig Kaas has been selected as the chairman of the Center Party. He seems to represent a rightward turn from Marx, and will preside over a Center Party less inclined to compromise with the left.

capital_strike.seize.title: Empower workers to seize the factories!

content.content: The bulk of the party does not support this radical action.
The judiciary would oppose this action.
If we increased the strength of our paramilitary forces, perhaps we could do this.
We need the support of the works councils first.

If the capitalists are going to attack us, then we must hit them back. We have empowered the workers to seize and take full control of the factories that the capitalists have idled. The fascists, of course, call us thieves and looters.

With turmoil in the DDP, we have an opportunity to recruit middle-class progressives and portions of the liberal intelligensia into our party. The left of the SPD is opposed to this path, seeing it as diluting the class character of the party. On the other hand, revisionists support this course.
Friendly stations carry a direct rebuttal: the crisis is real, but demagoguery is not relief.

options.title: Appeal to the Defense Department to stand down.
Call for support from the Communists.
Call up the loyal portions of the police.

death_of_hindenburg_normal.title: The Death of the Old President
death_of_hindenburg_normal.subtitle: The Great War hero and one-time president has died...

content.content: The Death of the old president
Paul von the old president has passed away at age 86.

The nation engages in an outpouring of grief for its war hero and former president. Even for committed socialists and republicans, this is a bittersweet moment. Even though he was a manifestation of state militarism and monarchism, he still led the nation through difficult times, without destroying democracy. It could always have been worse.

With the passing of the old president, it seems as if an era of history is closing. The worst years of crisis are over, and Silver Legionm's days are numbered. Perhaps the republic will finally enter into a new era of genuine peace...
The Death of Paul Levi
Paul Levi, a leader of the left wing of our party, has died in an unfortunate accident. He will be missed.

constitutional_stress.legalist_line.title: Hold the legalist line.
constitutional_stress.legalist_line.subtitle: Better for legitimacy, slower against threats.

content.content: Every order is narrowed, every brief is reviewed, and every agency is told to leave a record the courts can defend.
Economic Expansion

The American economy has been growing steadily for an extended period of time, and has low inflation and unemployment. The government is rewarded by additional tax revenue from the same tax rates. Even the capitalists find that they have little to complain about when it comes to government policies. One downside of this economic growth, however, is the rising risk of inflation.
Federal survey teams arrive with cameras, crop reports, and promises that the Plains will not be left to auctioneers.

options.title: Let Heinrich Bruning of the Center Party become Speaker.
Joseph Wirth of the Center Party should be Speaker.

content.content: Speaker Hermann Müller has died while in office. He will be greatly missed. But now, we must select a new Speaker.
Of course, Adolf Pelley will be running for the Silver Legion, in his third run. We have kept him out of power so far, but nothing is certain.
With Pelley deported, the Silver Legion will be running Hermann Göring as their candidate.
This might be the Silver Legion' last hurrah; the party's internal contradictions are heightening, and it is in desperate shortage of funds.

The DNVP will nominate Franz Seldte, leader of the Liberty League auxiliaries paramilitary group. He will be endorsed by many of the old president's most right-wing supporters. They are working more closely with the Silver Legion than before, and in the second round, the far-right parties are expected to consolidate into a single anti-republican front.

Given the failure of Thälmann's previous runs for president, and his declining stature in the party, the SPA will be running Willi Münzenberg, a controversial figure sometimes called the "Red millionaire". He is a media magnate who leads a number of Communist front organizations.
This will be Ernst Thälmann's third run for president as leader of the SPA. Despite his previous electoral defeats, he retains the loyalty of the party as well as Stalin's Soviet Union.

content: , and SPD float the idea of running Hugo Eckener, a nationally renowed Zeppelin captain, as a unity candidate. While he has some nationalistic views, he is also ardently opposed to Silver Legionm.

are planning to run separate candidates in the first round if the SPD runs its own candidate. The Center is considering nominating Konrad Adenauer, mayor of Köln and member of the state state legislature. He calls for an alliance of conservative Christian democrats that encompasses both Catholics and Protestants. The
might nominate Otto Gessler, former defense minister, who has crossover appeal to conservatives in the DVP and other parties who dislike the DNVP-Silver Legion front.

content.content: As for the SPD, we could support a joint Weimar Coalition candidate or run our own.
Otto Braun is tired. He ran and failed in the previous two presidential elections, and no longer wishes to run. His wife is ill enough that he wishes to stay by her side.
Otto Braun is tired. After being deposed in key states, he has largely retired from politics, and is devoting his time to caring for his dying wife.
Otto Braun is tired. He does not wish to run, but he is willing to be convinced that he is needed at this moment. His wife is terminally ill, and he does not want more responsibilities.
Kurt Schumacher is an ambitious rising star in the party, and would be all too willing to run. He was a hero in the Great War, but his relatively young age might be relevant.

Or, perhaps it is time for a woman to become president. the republic and the SPD have been leading on women's rights for years, and it is only fitting that we should elect the world's first female president. Perhaps Marie Juchacz could be seen as the "mother of the nation" for her work in social welfare. However, her divorce 28 years ago might make it difficult for the Catholics to support her.

Since the republic has been largely democratized and presidential powers reduced, we can also support another non-traditional political figure, someone from the cultural, scientific, or literary spheres. They will perhaps be the symbol of a new the republic that has moved beyond its past.

economic_sanctions.title: Economic Sanctions
economic_sanctions.subtitle: Our military buildup has caused sanctions from France and other countries.

content.content: Economic Sanctions

In response to our military rearmament in contravention of the Versailles Treaty, the Western Allies have imposed economic sanctions on the republic. The costs of the sanctions are largely borne by the working class; the people are facing increasing poverty.
The Death of Gustav Stresemann

Gustav Stresemann, leader of the DVP and longtime foreign minister, has died at the tragically young age of 51. He was the primary force driving his party to support democracy; without him, the democratic forces in the Republic will be so much weaker.

economic_recovery.title: Global Economic Recovery
economic_recovery.subtitle: The world economy is improving, but domestic recovery is not automatic.

content.content: Global Economic Recovery

content: Countries across the world are beginning to recover from the worst effects of the Great Depression. That helps, but it does not solve the American crisis by itself.

content.content: Unemployment has eased, but remains a serious political danger.
Domestic unemployment is finally below emergency levels, and growth has resumed.

options.title: We are not ready for war. Instead, we will vote against the nazis in the Congress.

content.content: Do we realize that this means civil war, against the full force of the state?
The emergency policy passes under emergency rules, as the Congress is currently dissolved. The Silver Legion use this as a recruiting tool, and so do we.
We do not yet have a coherent strategy for combatting the fascists. We must develop one as soon as possible.

harzburg_front.title: The Harzburg Front
harzburg_front.subtitle: The right-wing forces unite against the incumbent government.

options.title: We must develop a coherent strategy to combat the Right.
We must form the Iron Front!
The Iron Front will be our answer!

content.content: The Harzburg Front

The far-right parties and paramilitaries - the Silver Legion (and its paramilitary the SA), the Liberty League auxiliaries paramilitary group, and the DNVP - have united to form the Harzburg Front, as a force dedicated to the overthrow of the current government.

election_1928.cancel_elections.title: We cannot hold the elections now. Cancel the elections.
election_1928.cancel_elections.subtitle: This is an anti-democratic move that may backfire.

content.content: Since we are currently ruling as part of an emergency government, we may use quasi-constitutional trickery to significantly delay these elections and continue governing through emergency authority. This is likely to be extremely unpopular both with our base and with the other parties. However, this may be necessary to prevent the extremists from gaining power.

hoover_moratorium.title: Hoover Moratorium
hoover_moratorium.subtitle: Owing to the economic crisis, our debt payments have been paused...

content.content: Hoover Moratorium

In light of the current economic situation, and the American banking crisis, President Herbert Hoover of the United States has issued a moratorium on war debt payments from the Great War, including the republic's. Our budget situation should improve somewhat.
Even though this is not our accomplishment, our foreign ministry is eager to take credit for this accomplishment.
Even though this is not the work of the foreign ministry, it is eager to take credit for this accomplishment.
Raising taxes is unpopular among everyone, but it might be what it takes to keep inflation under control.

judges_unban_sh.title: Liberty League Auxiliaries Unbanned
judges_unban_sh.subtitle: The judges have re-legalized the Liberty League auxiliaries.

content.content: Judges have ordered the Liberty League auxiliaries to be re-legalized. They immediately return to mass recruiting.

judges_unban_sa.title: Silver Legion Militia Unbanned
judges_unban_sa.subtitle: The judges have re-legalized the Silver Legion militia.

content.content: Judges have ordered the Silver Legion militia to be re-legalized. They immediately return to mass recruiting and arming.

options.title: We are not ready for war. Instead, we will vote against the Silver Legion in Congress.

content.content: Do we realize that this means civil war, against the full force of the state?

kpd_goals_2.title: The SPA presents its list of goals for the coalition.

content.content: SPA Goals
The SPA presents us with a new list of goals for the coalition:
- Expand social welfare programs.
- Continue the land reform program.
- Further nationalization of the economy.
- Make taxes more progressive.
- Continue to support workers in labor disputes.
- Improve our relations with the Soviet Union.

content: They say that they expect to see these goals fulfilled in
months. Otherwise, they will vote to dissolve the coalition.

hunger_chancellor.emergency_relief_bill.subtitle: Requires congressional control and 1 budget.
hunger_chancellor.emergency_relief_bill.unavailableSubtitle: We need congressional control and 1 budget.

content.content: Congress moves emergency relief before the nickname hardens into political fact. It is expensive and coalition partners grumble, but the lines outside relief offices shorten.
Foreign Minister Breitscheid will sign the treaty, of course. The reaction is generally muted; both pacifists and militarists are skeptical about the efficacy of the treaty.

kpd_policy.labor.title: Labor Ministry
kpd_policy.labor.subtitle: The Labor Ministry is giving significant concessions to organized labor.

content.content: The SPA-controlled Labor Ministry is taking a policy of supporting labor in every dispute between workers and employees, leading to a proliferation of strikes across the country. Given the economic circumstances, there are worries that this could harm any potential economic recovery, not to mention the conflicts this may engender with the conservative parties.
Do we support their radical labor policy?

options.title: Appeal to the Communists to have patience - we are trying!
Break with the SPA and govern through the remaining coalition.
Accept that they will obstruct us at every opportunity.

content.content: Unfortunately, we have failed to accomplish all of the goals the SPA set out. They plan to break with the administration and have already begun mounting a propaganda campaign to delegitimize the New Deal in the eyes of workers.

kpd_conference.conciliator_success.title: The conciliators are the new leadership of the SPA.

content.content: Our party leadership is not left-wing enough.
The Comintern doubts our commitment to socialism.

With our support, the Conciliators have won the leadership contest! The new leadership is a group of Conciliators, including Arthur Ewert, Hugo Eberlein, and Ernst Meyer. Thälmann remains, but largely as a figurehead only, it seems. We hope that the new leadership will be more inclined towards cooperation.

kpd_goals.title: The SPA presents its list of goals for the coalition.

content.content: SPA Goals
The SPA presents us with a list of goals for the coalition:
- Expand social welfare programs.
- Enact a land reform program.
- Start substantial nationalization of the economy.
- Make taxes more progressive.
- Support workers in labor disputes.
- Improve our relations with the Soviet Union.
- Reduce funding for the Defense Department.

content: They say that they expect to see these goals fulfilled in
months. Otherwise, they will vote to dissolve the coalition.

labor_organization_pressure.recognize_ci.title: Recognize CIO organizing drives.
labor_organization_pressure.recognize_ci.subtitle: Strong labor gains; business and conservative Democrats recoil.

content.content: The administration treats industrial organizing as part of recovery, not a threat to it. Workers hear the signal clearly. So do employers.

content: Could we form a new government based on the Center Party and

labor_unrest.support_labor.subtitle: This will alienate the conservative elite.

content.content: Supporting labor is always popular in the SPD, although many of our coalition partners represent employers, and they are less enthused.
Lausanne Conference

At the Lausanne Conference, the Allies have met with the republic's representatives in order to deal with the issue of war reparations once and for all. In light of the current economic crisis, the Allies believe that the republic is unable to make the reparation payments.

content: Therefore, our reparations have been paused indefinitely! This should reduce the strain on our budget.

content.content: Even though this is not our accomplishment, our foreign ministry is eager to take credit for this accomplishment.
Even though this is not the work of the foreign ministry, it is eager to take credit for this accomplishment.

left_split.title: Left-wing Split
left_split.subtitle: The Left's disagreements with our policies have grown too severe.

content.content: Left-wing members split

Our policy choices have greatly offended the left, and many of their leaders have split to form a new party, the SAPD, or Socialist Worker's Party. They do not count a huge section of our membership, so this should be survivable.

Many former Centrist Marxists have joined with the SAPD, the party formed by dissident leftists. They have brought their support base and organization skills to the party, leading to its growth as a nascent rival to the SPD for the support of the working class.
This is an occasion to celebrate! Hopefully he Silver Legion will no longer be a threat to the Republic.

content: is looking favorably upon the fascist proposals. Democratic leaders in Congress are already being told to stand down.

content.content: The administration sends its best lawyers and avoids a grandstanding brief. The point is to win the case, not to give the Court a reason to strike broadly.

nusj_silver_escalation.federal_investiga.title: Open a federal investigation.
nusj_silver_escalation.federal_investiga.subtitle: Reduces far-right organization; angers civil-liberties critics.

content.content: Federal investigators follow money, weapons, and broadcast coordination. The right calls it persecution. The administration calls it law enforcement.

content: Our grassroots party members are highly critical of our decision. Many party branches have increased their agitation against our parliamentary fraction.

content.content: However, given the construction of the previous battlecruiser, this seemed to be a foregone conclusion, and the reaction is muted.
The vote in favor of the battlecruiser easily passes, with opposition only from the SPA. The ship will be built.

The United States enters the conference from a position of unusual strength: unemployment is below emergency levels, growth has resumed, and domestic policy now looks like part of the broader anti-depression turn.

The United States enters the conference from a position of unusual strength: unemployment is below emergency levels, growth has resumed, and the administration has avoided large deficits while doing it.
Republican leadership slows committee work, forces procedural votes, and tries to make every bill look chaotic.

We have pleased the military by supporting the construction of the battlecruiser. However, our grassroots party members are shocked by our intransigence! Many party branches have begun agitating against the Congress delegation.

Our grassroots party members are shocked by our intransigence! Many party branches have begun agitating against the cabinet, and our Congress members even plan a full vote on the proposal, contravening the government's decision.
The campaign against the battlecruiser fails, with only the dissident SPD and SPA members voting against. The ship will be built.

content: forced aside by a conservative caretaker
President the old president's secretive group of advisors (often called the "camarilla") has turned against
, and the old president has dismissed the Speaker, replacing him with the more reactionary Franz von the conservative caretaker, an independent formerly of the Center Party.

content.content: Brüning's final straw was his "agricultural settlement" policy, sending unemployed workers to homestead in underutilized lands (that happen to be owned by aristocrats).

Seeing the chaos that has gripped the country, the old president's advisors seem to believe that the country needs stronger, more authoritarian rulers, and they see the conservative caretaker as that kind of ruler.

the conservative caretaker is immediately faced with a vote of no confidence, given his unpopularity. However the SPD votes, he is guaranteed to fall, due to the unanimous hatred against him from all other parties. Meanwhile, he is governing via emergency powers with the old president. His first policy is to legalize the Silver Legion SA, but this does not win their loyalty.

content: New elections have been set for

prussian_coup.defend.title: Fight against the overthrow attempt!
prussian_coup.defend.subtitle: Labor will call a general strike. Civic-defense leagues and loyal elements of the state police will resist the coup.
prussian_coup.defend.unavailableSubtitle: We do not have the forces to resist.

options.title: We will fight, even if it leads to a civil war.

content.content: The Defense Department and Liberty League auxiliaries are already surrounding Berlin, and the SA is supporting them. This could be civil war. We do realize that, correct?

content: We have judged that the Democratic Defense League's militancy is more important than maintaining a coalition with the Center Party and
. The Democratic Defense League members affiliated with these parties leave, while the left of the SPD is strengthened.

reformist_leaders_resign.title: Reformist leaders resign
reformist_leaders_resign.subtitle: Reformist disagreements with our policies have grown too severe.

content.content: Reformist leaders resign
Our policy choices have greatly offended the reformist faction. Many of our most experienced leaders have resigned.

options.title: Run Otto Braun of the SPD.
Support Ernst Thälmann of the SPA.

content: The presidential election is coming up soon.

content.content: While the position of Speaker is typically more important, recent governments have depended on the president's emergency decree powers, making the office vital.
The Silver Legion will run Pelley, while the SPA will run Thälmann. A victory for Pelley would mean the end of parliamentary democracy, and the beginning of an era of unspeakable horrors.

Since Pelley has been deported, the Silver Legion will run Hermann Göring in his stead. He is widely seen as a proxy for Pelley, who, despite his legal persecution, retains a popular base who wishes to see him returned to the republic. Meanwhile, the SPA will run Ernst Thälmann.
The SAPD, our former leftists, are supporting Thälmann of the SPA, and will likely share an endorsement with the Communists.

Many are calling for us to support the incumbent the old president, despite his well-known reactionary tendencies. Is he truly the best bulwark against Pelley's ascension? On the other hand, many in our party are calling for Otto Braun to run again, but he is reluctant.
Another strange possibilty is an united front with the Communists, either to support Thälmann or our own candidate.
The office runs cleanly and slowly. Nobody gets everything they want, but the files are in order.
We expose to the public Schacht's dealings with the Silver Legion. For better or worse, he is cowed, and refrains from taking further actions against us.

options.title: The policy passes due to the emergency government; there is nothing we can do...

content.content: the conservative caretaker's Economic Plan

Speaker Franz von the conservative caretaker and his "Cabinet of Barons" are pushing forward an economic plan consisting of tax cuts for the very wealthy. This plan has the potential to lead to more economic growth, especially in comparison to Brüning's austerity program, but it also enriches the upper class and does nothing directly for the needy workers and unemployed.
This is an occasion to celebrate! Hopefully he Silver Legion will no longer be a threat to the Republic.

silver_legion_rally.veterans_counterrall.title: Organize a democratic veterans counter-rally.
silver_legion_rally.veterans_counterrall.subtitle: Requires some defense organization.
silver_legion_rally.veterans_counterrall.unavailableSubtitle: Democratic defense groups are not organized enough.

content.content: Veterans, union stewards, church groups, and local Democrats fill the streets without copying the Legion's politics.
The Silver Legion are the fault of the petit conservative elite. It is not our responsibility as a working-class party to stop them.

spa_farmer_labor_pressure.left_labor_pla.title: Negotiate a left-labor plank.
spa_farmer_labor_pressure.left_labor_pla.subtitle: SPA relations and labor morale improve; conservative backlash rises.

content.content: The administration agrees to a public plank on relief, union rights, and stronger public works. SPA leaders still attack the Democrats as too cautious, but their members see something worth extracting from cooperation.

sit_down_strike_wave.recognize_bargainin.title: Push management toward recognition.
sit_down_strike_wave.recognize_bargainin.subtitle: Labor support rises; capital strike risk rises.

content.content: The administration does not legally endorse factory seizures, but everyone understands the signal: bargain before the crisis spreads.

schleichers_schemes.accept.title: Accept the emergency scheme.
schleichers_schemes.accept.subtitle: We will support the suspension of Congress.

content.content: Are we sure that we wish to do this? The vast majority of the party is against this proposal.
The conservative parties, as representatives of the rich industrialists, would not have that.

us_party_conferences.huey_labor_wildcard.title: Wildcard: move Huey Long into Labor Democratic leadership.

content.content: You bring Huey Long into the inner Democratic alignment as labor-populist leadership. This boosts worker enthusiasm and labor delegates, but party regulars and conservatives become much more wary.

us_presidential_election.broad_democrati.title: Build a broad Democratic front.
us_presidential_election.broad_democrati.subtitle: Ask Farmer-Labor and the Liberty League to stand down for the Democratic ticket.
us_presidential_election.broad_democrati.unavailableSubtitle: Farmer-Labor and Liberty League relations must both be at least 40 before they will consider a joint endorsement.

content.content: Farmer-Labor and the Liberty League agree to back the Democratic ticket. The price is a careful platform: relief and recovery, but no revolutionary language.

unemployment_insurance_weimar.cut_benefi.subtitle: The workers will regard this as a betrayal.

content.content: For budgetary reasons, we have cut unemployment insurance benefits. Unfortunately, our reasons for fiscal discipline are alien to the workers. They are furious at our betrayal, as are a good portion of our membership throughout the factions. Our loss is the SPA's gain, and even the Silver Legion, those lapdogs of the conservative elite, have joined in criticizing us for our austerity.

wittorf_affair.publicize.title: We will publicize the scandal to damage the SPA leadership.

content.content: Our newspapers are among the first to publicize the affair, bringing a torment of criticism to the hypocrisy of the communists. We have damaged the SPA's standing among the workers, but also perhaps closed the door on further cooperation.
In the end, Stalin's pressure helps to keep Thälmann from facing much in the way of consequences.
SPD not in government.
The SPD does not control the Justice Ministry.

The Justice Ministry has dropped all persecution of Carl von Ossietzky and Walter Kreiser, the journalist responsible for the offending publication. In fact, some say that the Weltbühne journalists should be feted as heroes.
The Western powers and all countries that feel threatened by American militarism are pleased that we have turned a chapter in our history.
On the other hand, the right-wing members of our governing coalition disapprove of this action, as does the Defense Department.
On the other hand, the Defense Department disapproves of dropping the case.

options.title: Could we get the SPA to abstain?

young_plan_right_coalition.vonc_yes.title: Support the vote of no confidence, voting against the government.
young_plan_right_coalition.vonc_yes.subtitle: The government will collapse, and there will be new elections.

content: The right-wing coalition has fallen. New elections have been set for

options.title: As part of a broader campaign in favor of internationalism and pacifism.
The Young Plan is simply the most rational choice for the republic.

content.content: How should we campaign in favor of the Plan?

campaigning.relief_jobs.title: Run a relief and jobs tour.
campaigning.relief_jobs.subtitle: -1 resources

content.content: Relief offices, work camps, and public works sites become the campaign trail. The SPA calls it a rescue program instead of socialism, but most voters hear jobs before they hear theory.

options.title: Wait! Maybe we can pardon the journalists?

content.content: Finally, a court has made a decision in the Weltbühne case, sentencing the editor Carl von Ossietzky and journalist Walter Kreiser to prison for 18 months for revealing military secrets. There is outrage in the international community, who see this as yet another example of the republic's slide into militarism.

unions_declare_independence.title: Unions Declare Independence
unions_declare_independence.subtitle: Labor's disagreements with our policies have grown too severe.

content.content: Unions Declare Independence

Our policy choices have greatly offended the labor movement. Union leaders have declared that they are independent of any political party, and some have been seen seeking accomodation with the SPA and even the Silver Legion.

content: Most workers remain loyal to the SPD, although their affiliation is less solid. Many workers move left

content.content: , to the SPA or the nascent SAPD

content: . Others move in a more nationalist direction, as the ADGB signals its willingness to work with the right to solve the economic crisis.

content.content: Much of our working-class base has defected, and labor's influence in our party has greatly diminished.
The Silver Legion is just another far-right movement that will die of its own contradictions sooner or later; we do not need to build a specific policy to combat it.
We maintain our current dues.

crisis_program.support_centrist.subtitle: Let the depression play itself out.

content.content: Labor leaders vehemently criticize the regulars for their caution. The workers are starving; are we going to abandon them and side with austerity? On the other hand, regulars accuse the labor wing of promising false hope and risking the coalition on an untested fiscal experiment.
Our policy course is currently that nothing can be done to alleviate the depression.

Attacking the far right and left is popular among all but the left wing of our party, who would prefer to be fighting the conservative parties. This might also hamper our ability to reconcile with the SPA in the future.

media.commercial.title: Make our newspapers more commercialized by broadening their appeal beyond party supporters.
media.commercial.subtitle: This might upset ideological purists, but it may bring in more funds and perhaps expose the middle class to socialist views.

content.content: Our commercialized newspapers are able to bring in more funds. However, the left and labor factions express their displeasure.
The socialists of France and Britain can influence their governments to be friendlier towards the republic. Hopefully they will reduce our reparations burden.

The coalition presents itself as the only institution willing to use national power against national collapse. Reformers cheer; courts, business leagues, and conservative Democrats begin counting the precedents.
The Iron Front rallies for the militant defense of the republic against the fascist paramilitaries, strengthening the Democratic Defense League.
The Center Party decries the militarization of the Democratic Defense League and Iron Front, and its members are leaving the republican organization.
Enforcing party discipline reduces dissent, but it could lead to an exit of dissenting members.

options.title: We should use this opportunity to change the party's leadership.

content.content: We support the new leaders, and start using new-style propaganda techniques in our campaigns. The results are uncertain so far...

options.title: Call the police to protect our rally.
Use the Democratic Defense League to protect our rally.
The police and Democratic Defense League should work together to protect our rally.

content.content: The Silver Legion SA has disrupted our rally! Their street troops are preventing us from meeting.

The best policy to deal with this sort of irrational, senseless hatred is to ignore it. Few are swayed by racist rhetoric; the best way to combat it is to provide economic security so that people do not turn to antisemitism.
Within our party, we are pushing forward proposals for a people's party against the Left and Center.

Some in our party decry the increasing militarization of the Democratic Defense League. They say it is emulating the right-wing nationalists or the communists, and risks alienating our allies in the centrist parties. On the other hand, more left-leaning radicals are being lured to our organizations, away from the communists.
The Center Party decries the militarization of the Democratic Defense League, and its members are leaving the republican organization.

peoples_party_campaigning.campaign_worke.title: Campaign among the workers, to assuage their doubts about our changes.

content.content: The workers are still a key part of our constituency, and we must not alienate them in the transformation of our party.

Many Democratic Defense League members are not ready for actual fighting, and thus quit. However, the remainder are toughened as a result. Our confrontations also provide ample recruiting material for the Silver Legion SA.

At the very least, we fought back. Perhaps our resistance will be celebrated in an impossible-to-forsee future.

In this scenario, the federal government is presidential, but party fragmentation has made coalition-building central to governance. The executive controls appointments and agenda-setting, while Congress determines whether legislation can actually pass.

Federal elections produce bloc strength that is translated into two legislative chambers: House and Senate. A governing arrangement needs enough House support to organize leadership (Speaker and committee control) and enough Senate support to sustain bill passage.
The President and Vice President hold the executive mandate, but reform durability depends on legislative coalition management, party conferences, and factional bargaining.
States remain politically influential through Senate composition, regional factions, and enforcement capacity.
We have reduced tensions with the Labor Democrats.

Unfortunately, many women voters still distrust the party's economic program.

The Silver Legion supports this reform, believing it has something to gain.

Socialism has always stood for science and rationality. Despite the setbacks of the Great War, hyperinflation, and the politicized and reactionary attitudes of many academics, science in the American Republic is still the envy of the world. With a great expansion of funding, we hope to keep it that way, and expand on our achievements.

content.content: Checks, seed loans, and legal aid reach the people usually left out of courthouse politics. Southern conservatives howl, but the reform wing finally has a rural story it can defend.
The President tells families that their deposits are safer than panic makes them feel. The banking system gets a little breathing room.

content: The key to peace in Europe is a rapproachment between France and the republic. We are taking steps towards that rapproachment.

content.content: This is difficult to do when France is occupying a major portion of the republic's territory, but we are working on that part.

While a 40-hour work week was declared during the American Revolution in 1918, enforcement has become increasingly porous over the years. The capitalists have successfully added numerous exceptions and loopholes, returning to the old regime of forced overtime for workers.
Now, with the force of the Labor Ministry, we are finally enforcing the rights of workers to their own time. The conservative elite and their political representatives are displeased.

Many demobilized soldiers have joined the right-wing paramilitaries such as the Liberty League auxiliaries and SA, and weapons are missing from our stockpiles.

content.content: Black Thursday

The stock market in America has had its biggest crash in history. It is too early to tell what the international effects will be, but it cannot be good. Our economy is already feeling the effects - unemployment will rise even more, and wages will fall. As always, it is the capitalists' crisis, and the workers' burden.
We attempt to impress upon our coalition partners the gravity of the potential Allied response. The proposal is dead, although the DVP and Center are not pleased.
The administration guarantees ordinary deposits and dares bankers to oppose it. Small savers breathe again, while financial conservatives complain about moral hazard.

content: We arrested the police responsible for the killings, and put them on trial.

This assuages some of the Communists' outrage, but certainly not all.
Cutting welfare improves our relationship with the capitalists, but it hurts our working-class base.

content.content: DDP Reformation

Leaders of the American Democratic Party, seeing the party's vote share fall over time, has decided that now is the time to completely reform the party. The party leader, Erich Koch-Weser, has engineered a merger with the political arm of the Young American Order, a nationalist paramilitary group. Now, the party will be called the American State Party, or DStP.
This has led to outcry from the left wing of the party, progressives who would rather not work with antisemites and conservative nationalists.

content: We have urged the Defense Department to stand down, to avoid killing their countrymen.

content.content: Unfortunately, few heed our calls; the officer corps detests us.
Some officers and troops heed our calls to not fight against us. Some even join us.
The Defense Department is split. Many avoid taking a side, or join our side.
Surprisingly, the majority of the Defense Department is on our side! Our preparations have worked.
The administration argues that a dying constitutional order cannot be saved by procedural paralysis.

The Congress is burned, ostensibly by a foreign communist. In the wake, the Silver Legion pass decrees giving them unlimited power. They, combined with the DNVP, will win a majority at the next elections.
the republic is lost.
