Economics
Endgame Settlements
The campaign now distinguishes several broad endings.
Democratic stabilization

content: means the New Deal has reduced economic crisis, preserved public legitimacy, and built enough constitutional support to govern through elections and bargaining.

content.content: Contested New Deal

content: means the administration survives, but recovery and institutional reform remain incomplete.

content.content: Managed reaction

content: means democracy survives only by narrowing the reform program under pressure from courts, business, conservative legislators, and coalition defectors.

content.content: Systemic rupture

content: means the republic technically survives, but constitutional stress, radical momentum, or civil conflict have made normal government unstable.

content.content: Authoritarian takeover

content: means reactionary forces have broken democratic government outright.

aufhauser.moderate_economic_plan.title: Support the Pragmatic New Deal plan
aufhauser.moderate_economic_plan.subtitle: Hillman can support a pragmatic jobs program if labor receives a seat at the table.

content.content: We have already adopted the Pragmatic New Deal plan.
We are not currently in an economic crisis.
Sidney Hillman
Hillman is a union organizer with strong ties to garment workers, industrial labor, and urban progressives. He can bridge labor militancy and practical New Deal administration.

hilferding.economic_democracy_.title: Economic Democracy
hilferding.economic_democracy_.subtitle: Enact policies to build a more democratic economy.

content.content: Democratic Party not in government.
We do not control the necessary departments.
Rexford Tugwell
Tugwell is one of the administration's planning-minded economists. He favors expert agencies, national economic coordination, and a New Deal that reshapes markets instead of merely patching them.
Hull must be Speaker.

content: We have called for new elections, set for

content.content: Cordell Hull
Hull is an experienced foreign-policy hand and party loyalist. He is strongest on trade, diplomacy, and keeping international commitments from becoming domestic political poison.

leber.build_peoples_party.title: Building a People's Party
leber.build_peoples_party.subtitle: Can we expand our party beyond the industrial working class?

content.content: There is not enough support for this policy within the party.

sender.crisis.title: Addressing the economic crisis
sender.crisis.subtitle: Bethune can help support a left-wing economic program.

content.content: We are not currently in an economic crisis.
We have already adopted the Structural Transformation plan.
Mary McLeod Bethune
Mary McLeod Bethune is an educator, organizer, and civil-rights advocate who can connect the New Deal to Black communities, working women, and the party's moral reform wing.

pfulf.womens_rights_.title: Women's rights
pfulf.womens_rights_.subtitle: Enact policy with regards to women's rights.

content.content: The Democratic Party is not currently in government.
Eleanor Roosevelt
Roosevelt is the Democratic coalition's most visible social reformer, linking women's clubs, settlement-house networks, labor organizers, and middle-class liberals into a broader New Deal majority.
The party press runs editorials in support of Party Regular positions.
Farley must be Speaker or President.

content: We have called for new elections, set for

content.content: James Farley
Farley is Roosevelt's party organizer and patronage chief. He keeps county chairs, state bosses, and campaign donors pointed in roughly the same direction.

content: Business-facing appointments lower the temperature with the

content.content: GOP

content: and the Liberty League. The left of the coalition reads it as retreat.

options.title: Put labor allies in the delivery agencies.
Reward party regulars and congressional managers.

content.content: Cabinet Personnel

Cabinet seats remain under the party that holds the presidency, but appointments still decide how the state behaves. A secretary can make a program swift, cautious, legalistic, or openly partisan without changing a single statute.

content: -2 budget - Create a state monopoly for grain purchases, guaranteeing prices for farmers and stable prices for consumers.

content.content: The judiciary would never allow this.

Our new purchasing agency guarantees fair prices for farmers, while also providing lower food prices for workers and the urban poor. This policy necessitates heavy spending and an unprecedented level of state intervention in the economy, raising the ire of conservative economists.

options.title: Do not enact any policies for now.

content.content: Agricultural Policy

While agricultural policy has historically not been the domain of the SPD, there is much need for policy in this area. Our urban constituencies hope for cheaper and more stable food supplies, while small farmers need economic relief. There is also the movement for land reform, to break up the large aristocratic estates and redistribute land to peasants.

We have successfully urged the coalition parties to increase social spending! Unfortunately, the center-right parties increasingly see us as a nuisance, and might not wish to continue these negotiations.

constitutional_reform.constructive_vonc.subtitle: Votes of no confidence can only be called if an alternative plan for government formation can be passed, greatly reducing their incidence.

options.title: Call a constitutional referendum to pass the reform!

content.content: Moderate coalition partners support this reform, which will greatly improve the stability of government.
Some liberals support this reform, but moderate coalition partners do not trust us enough to support the constitutional referendum.

content: Conservative, radical right, and SPA legislators are in opposition, as they believe the current system gives them leverage.

content.content: Unfortunately, our forces fail to disloge the SA from their positions surrounding Pelley. He is seen as both a strong and powerful leader and a victim, further reinforcing his image. The Silver Legion are left stronger than ever.
Roads, depots, schools, flood control, and relief offices make wavering members suddenly practical. The bill gets easier; the budget gets uglier.

coalition_affairs.fdr_personal.subtitle: Uses presidential prestige.
coalition_affairs.fdr_personal.unavailableSubtitle: New Deal legitimacy must be at least 45.

content.content: Roosevelt intervenes directly with faction leaders, governors, committee chairs, and radio allies. The coalition holds, but personal authority is a finite resource.

education_science.applied.title: Fund applied research in economically useful technologies.
education_science.applied.subtitle: -1 budget

content.content: Our research program focuses on collaborating with industry to develop useful technologies, creating new research institutions for applied science. This collaboration is focused on economic growth and development.
Banning the Liberty League auxiliaries weakens our relationship with the old conservative establishment, the political right, and the police.
Banning the Liberty League auxiliaries weakens our relationship with the political right and the police.
It also weakens our coalition due to conservative influence on the right wing of our coalition.

content: In addition, the auxiliaries still operate out of public sight.

economic_democracy.nationalize_1.title: Begin to socialize key industries.
economic_democracy.nationalize_1.subtitle: -1 budget - we should buy out industries and govern them together with workers and consumers.

content.content: We have begun to socialize key industries by buying out the capitalist owners, and creating coordinated governing boards consisting of worker, consumer, and state representatives. The capitalists are not pleased at our intrusion into the economy.
Cheap credit through existing banks calms lenders and helps some farmers survive. It is administratively easy, politically modest, and not nearly enough for the most desperate counties.

options.title: Pay growers to cut output and raise prices.
Direct relief toward tenants, sharecroppers, and farm laborers.
Offer cheap credit through conservative farm banks.

content.content: Farm Crisis

Farm prices have collapsed, foreclosures spread through the Plains, and every auction risks becoming a political rally. Relief for agriculture can stabilize the countryside, but every route creates a different opposition.

fireside_chat.call_for_labor_peace.title: Call for labor peace with rights.

content.content: The President asks employers to bargain and workers to organize responsibly. Nobody gets everything, which is why the message lands.
Fireside Chat
Radio lets the President speak past newspapers, party machines, and opposition rumor campaigns. The message matters.

foreign_policy.improve_soviet.title: Improve our relations with the Soviet Union.
foreign_policy.improve_soviet.subtitle: We could also broach the question of the SPA...

options.title: Provide aid, in exchange for Defense Department assistance.
Provide aid, in exchange for only good relations.

content.content: The Soviet Union also suffered from the international order created by the Treaty of Versailles; they have been participating in our secret rearmament program. The Treaty of Rapallo in 1922 affirmed our friendly relations.

The Soviets are asking for economic and developmental assistance. They are still a poor country on the whole, and are desperately trying to catch up to the western powers. There is a chance that our anti-communist coalition partners would be upset, but we could assuage them by asking the Soviets for secret assistance in training and equiping the Defense Department.

economic_policy.wtb_review_mature.title: Review Share Our Wealth results and tighten administration.
economic_policy.wtb_review_mature.subtitle: Administrative follow-through without a new appropriation.
economic_policy.wtb_review_mature.unavailableSubtitle: Unemployment is low enough that the emergency works drive has passed.

content.content: The administration audits the works offices, shifts crews toward projects that are already moving, and uses the program's existing machinery to keep employment rising. It does not have the force of a new appropriation, but it shows results without asking Congress for more money.

fiscal_policy.raise_poor.title: Raise consumption taxes, which disproportionately affect the poor.
fiscal_policy.raise_poor.subtitle: +2 budget
fiscal_policy.raise_poor.unavailableSubtitle: Tax rates are already too high.

content.content: We have raised taxes for the poor and working class. This hurts our support among the workers.

judicial_crisis.defy_court.title: Publicly denounce the Court and keep pushing.
judicial_crisis.defy_court.subtitle: Mobilizes the base, damages legitimacy.

content.content: The administration declares that no court can be allowed to freeze the nation in depression. The base cheers; constitutional moderates recoil.
Supreme Court Challenge
Major New Deal measures are moving toward the courts. Business attorneys, Liberty League donors, and conservative judges argue that the administration has exceeded federal power.

labor_movement.state_neutrality.title: Stay formally neutral.
labor_movement.state_neutrality.subtitle: Avoids a fight, wastes an opening.

content.content: The administration refuses to choose sides. Employers are relieved; labor organizers conclude that neutrality favors whoever already holds power.
Labor Movement Strategy

The labor movement is reorganizing under pressure from unemployment, industrial conflict, and the New Deal. The administration can favor conservative craft unionism, back industrial organizing, or try to mediate.

content: The Labor Ministry works on developing and enforcing safety regulations, in order to ensure healty working conditions for the working class.

content.content: Businesses have begun to argue that our focus on safety is overbearing and unnecessary, interfering with the economy.
Unfortunately, our investigations have failed to turn up anything. Rumors are that the targets of our investigation were tipped off by infiltrators within the police.
We have kept welfare spending the same.

content: As it turns out, the Silver Legion SA has many allies in the police and bureaucracy. Our relationship with the police weakens, and the SA can still organize illegally, right under the police's noses.

content.content: Nevertheless, the SA is substantially weakened by our ban.
Federal auditors and emergency administrators route aid around local obstruction. The program becomes cleaner and more national, while constitutional lawyers begin sharpening their knives.

options.title: Lean on Democratic governors to build a national patronage machine.
Offer regional compacts to progressive governors and Farmer-Labor administrations.
Let southern courthouse machines administer relief with few questions.
Send federal auditors and administrators around hostile statehouses.

content.content: State Governors
Relief now runs through statehouses as much as through Washington. Governors can sell a program, sabotage it, or turn it into a rival machine before the federal agencies even print the forms.
Democratic

content: governors want patronage and visible projects.

content.content: Farmer-Labor

content: governors want farm and labor concessions. Conservative state machines want money without transformation.

content.content: This is unlikely to succeed; we do not have majority support.

all_quiet.everyone.unavailableSubtitle: The police are unable to guarantee the safety of all the attendees.

content.content: Under police protection, crowds throng the film's showings, as Silver Legion protest outside. The reception is mixed, but a new national conversation develops, reexamining the right-wing histories of the war. Quietly, many minds are changed, or at least affected in subtle ways.

all_quiet.title: All Quiet on the Western Front
all_quiet.subtitle: Censorship and violence over a movie...

options.title: Ban the film, to prevent Silver Legion violence.
Allow the film to be shown.
Allow the film to be shown, and use the state police to prevent Silver Legion disruptions.
In fact, everyone needs to learn the lessons of this film. Give free tickets to students and government employees.

content.content: All Quiet on the Western Front
All Quiet on the Western Front
Im Westen nichts Neues

content: by Erich Maria Remarque, is a film about the experiences of American soldiers in the Great War, and how they gradually became disillusioned with the ideas of patriotism and nationalism.

content.content: As an anti-war film, it is the target of major protest by far-right actors, especially the Silver Legion and the Liberty League auxiliaries. They are calling for the government to ban the film. If we do not ban it, the SA threatens to violently stop the film from being shown or watched.

When the march arrives in Altona, they are immediately confronted by an angry crowd of communists and socialists, who throw objects at the Silver Legion. The police attempt to separate the two groups, to no avail. Gunshots are heard, reportedly from rooftop snipers. content.content: Suspension of the Austrian Parliament

Due to a controversial proposal regarding a railroad strike in the Austrian National Council, all three presidents of the National Council resigned, leaving it without any presiding officers. This is a constitutional crisis. Austrian speaker Engelbert Dollfuß has taken advantage of this crisis by suspending the parliament and ruling by emergency decree, forming an "Austrofascist" government. Dollfuß consolidates his power by persecuting his political opponents, especially our fellow Social Democrats in the SDAPÖ (Social Democratic Worker's Party of Austria).
Is there anything we could do?

Because of our government's policies, which are perceived as anti-business, the capitalists are reducing their investment and laying off workers. This has the immediate effect of raising unemployment and dissent against our government, especially among the middle class.

Capital Strike

Our policies have greatly angered the capitalists. Investment has fallen, and factories are idling, preventing the workers from fulfilling their jobs. Meanwhile, the conservative press is a constant chorus of venomous invective directed at us and our economic policies.
What can we do?

understanding_enemy.title: Understanding the Enemy
understanding_enemy.subtitle: Why did the Silver Legion gain so many votes?

options.title: The Silver Legion are the fault of the petit conservative elite. It is not our responsibility as a working-class party to stop them.
We can combat the Silver Legion by adopting a "People's Party" posture that might compete for support among the middle class.
We should use new Mierendorff's new propaganda methods to fight the the Silver Legion.
We must focus our work among the working class to prevent Silver Legion in-roads.

content.content: Understanding the Enemy
The Silver Legion' rise is unprecedented for a political party in modern the republic, and socialist intellectuals are doing their best to come to terms with the rapid rise of this dangerous party.

The trade union researcher Theodor Geiger has written an article titled "Panik im Mittelstand", in which he describes the Silver Legion as being primarily a movement of the middle class, especially lower-middle civil servants and professionals who fear the proletarianizing effects of the Depression. Geiger argues that the middle class has been economically and politically unmoored, long split between many competing parties, squeezed between the big businesses and the working class. The mix of nationalist, anti-democratic, and loosely anti-capitalist rhetoric of the Silver Legion appeals to this group, perhaps only temporarily before the middle class discovers its true class interests.

By contrast, the theoretician Alexander Schifrin understands the Silver Legion as a new, unprecedented type of political movement, a fascist party that has non-economic appeal across class lines. He calls to attention its use of violent propaganda and street demonstrations as central to their rise.

Carlo Mierendorff, a leader of the "new revisionists", understands the Silver Legion as a politically incoherent but nevertheless powerful movement. They are kept together by the strength of their organizing machine and the emotional appeal of their nebulous ideology, which is able to attract less informed voters who voted based on feeling. Countering them would require a new style of campaigning, focused more on symbols and emotion rather than reason.
It is possible that all or none of these hypotheses are correct. Whatever the case, there must be something we can do to stop their rise.
Unfortunately, the coalition supporting the dissolution of the Congress does not constitute a majority. There will be elections as scheduled.
Fine. If the conservative elite wish to blow up the government, let them do so. Socialists will not be complicit in the destruction of the working class.
Heinrich Brüning of the Center Party has been appointed Speaker.

content: Brüning now leads an all-conservative cabinet. Using the emergency powers of Article 48, Brüning and President the old president have passed the welfare cuts over the objections of the Congress.

content.content: The Weltbühne Trial

The left-wing news and literary magazine Weltbühne has been targeted by the national authorities for allegedly revealing state secrets. The magazine published articles alleging a secret rearmament program by the Defense Department, in contravention of the Treaty of Versailles. The military authorities obviously do not want this information to be known. It does not help that the magazine's editor, Carl von Ossietzky, is a well-known pacifist.
With our control of the Justice Ministry, we could simply drop the case.
The Justice Ministry is seeking to persecute the editor and journalist of Weltbühne. We can try to persuade our coalition partners to drop the case, but they will likely resist.
Fine. We enacted a massive austerity program to cut welfare, in order to maintain the stability of our coalition. The vote has been called off.

The referendum easily fails with or without our presence in the campaign, with only around 15% of voters supporting it. However, nationalist sentiment is inflammed, and the right-wing parties are using our silence around the Plan as a tacit admission of its faults. Our silence has only emboldened the Right, especially the Silver Legion.

options.title: Campaign in favor of the Young Plan and against the referendum.
Do not campaign on the Young Plan, one way or another.

content.content: Young Plan

The Young Plan is an agreement with the Western powers to reduce the republic's reparation payments and give us more fiscal autonomy. Simultaneously, France will agree to withdraw its military occupation from the Rhineland.

While this seems to be a positive development for the country, the Right is campaigning hard against this plan, as are the Communists for whatever reason. They claim that any reparations are a form of national slavery, and have organized a referendum against it.

Since most proletarians cannot afford family radios right now, we are reaching the middle class, a fact of which our labor comrades are painfully aware.

The Stalinists of the SPA insists on calling us "social fascists", suggesting that we social democrats are as bad or worse than actual fascists. Needless to say, this is not good for our relationship with the Communists. By observing the Comintern, we can better understand the SPA, enabling us to potentially improve relations, or counter the SPA if it may come to that.

As part of normalizing relations with the SPA, we have to acknowledge the wrongdoings of our past. We have expelled Noske, and have held public commemorations for those killed by troops during the uprisings.
This leads to tensions with the moderates and reformists within our party, and criticism from the conservative and liberal parties.
The coalition insists that recovery must be orderly, constitutional, and reassuring to property. It buys breathing room with courts and business, while the left wonders what the landslide was for.

As a people's party, the urban poor are still a key part of our constituency.

Farley has been removed as an advisor. The Party Regulars disapprove.

The New Deal has survived its first constitutional test. Unemployment is falling, the republic retains enough public legitimacy to govern, and the administration has built institutions that can outlast the first emergency.

This is not a clean victory. The courts, conservative Democrats, labor militants, the SPA, and the radical right all remain part of national politics. But the republic now has a working path through crisis: elections, bargaining, relief, and reform instead of collapse.

The Silver Legion SA is planning a march through Altona, a working-class neighborhood of Hamburg known for its high working-class population. Altona is a Communist stronghold, and the planned march will almost certainly lead to violent conflict.

austrian_civil_war.force_peace.title: Can we bring the sides together for a peace?

content.content: The Austrian government obviously sees us as biased, so there is no way they will let us host peace negotiations. However, negotiations begin nonetheless. In Switzerland, with Fascist Italy as the Austrofascists' protector and the republic as the SDAPÖ's, a peace deal is hammered out. The socialists are disarmed and give up all pretense of revolution, while the right-wing paramilitaries are also disarmed. Dictatorship ends, and normal government is restored, with free elections.
Given the SDAPÖ's starting position, this is treated as a victory. Now begins the long work of reconstruction...
Unfortunately, the far-right parties united with the government to outvote us. The customs union will be formed.

However, the Allies, especially France, are likely to object. This agreement would break the terms of the Versailles Treaty, and could lead to economic sanctions, further hurting our economy.

schleichers_schemes.title: Conservative Emergency Schemes
schleichers_schemes.subtitle: Conservative operators claim to have a plan to save the republic...

content.content: the conservative operator's Schemes

Speaker Kurt von the conservative operator claims to have a plan to save the republic, and he has reached out to the Social Democrats with vague promises. He claims that in order to govern, the Congress must be temporarily suspended to prevent the Silver Legion and Communists from disrupting government, in effect giving him and the old president dictatorial powers. In the meantime, he is enacting a public works program and job creation plan, which he claims will stabilize the economy,

The trade unions are somewhat favorable to the conservative operator's plan, as they see a commonality between the conservative operator's economic plan and the ADGB's public works plan for debt-funded job creation. However, the rest of our party is vehemently against the conservative operator.

Rumors are that the conservative operator is also reaching out to the Silver Legion with similar promises of cooperation; he seems to believe that he can "tame" the Silver Legion. Most in our party do not trust him to not bring the Silver Legion to power. In addition, working with him would essentially mean the end of our claim to be a "democratic" party.

Unfortunately, we must accept this ultimatum if we are to maintain this coalition. The Silver Legion and Communists are waiting in the wings for us to fail, ready to pounce upon the instability that would result from a breakup of the governing coalition.

The workers are furious at our betrayal, as are a good portion of our membership throughout the factions. Our loss is the SPA's gain, and even the Silver Legion, those lapdogs of the conservative elite, have joined in criticizing us for our austerity.

vote_of_no_confidence.resources.unavailableSubtitle: We do not have enough resources.
vote_of_no_confidence.resources.subtitle: -3 resources

content.content: By transferring resources to our coalition partners, we can reduce their dissent against our policies. This is a crude, but effective solution.

The campaign makes the New Deal a defense of constitutional democracy against Coughlinite radio and Silver Legion intimidation. It strengthens the center and alienates radicals who think the administration is hiding behind legalism.

options.title: Run a relief and jobs tour.
Promise middle-class security and clean administration.
Make labor rights the campaign centerpiece.
We either don't want to or cannot campaign right now.

content.content: Campaigning
Whether or not an election is near, the party has to explain what the New Deal is for. A campaign line can win voters, but it also tells other parties what kind of bargain the Democrats are offering.

crisis_program.support_left.subtitle: Call for mass nationalizations and the transformation of the economy.
crisis_program.support_left.unavailableSubtitle: The New Deal Left is not strong enough.

content.content: Our anti-conservative rhetoric is popular among the left, but may make it harder to build the coalitions that are necessary for government in the future.

options.title: The anti-democratic parties of the far right and left - the SPA, Silver Legion, and DNVP.
The far right - the Silver Legion and DNVP.
The conservative parties - the Center and DVP.

content.content: Choosing our enemies
In our media and campaign materials, who should we consider as our main opponents?

content: as our prior enemies. Is a change of direction necessary?

media.radio_2.title: We must keep on building up our radio network.
media.radio_2.subtitle: -1 resources

content.content: Our new radio network has a mix of popular and political programming, and is proving a success.
The Socialist Party of America is tiny and has limited support. There is not much we can do for them, and conversely, not much they can do for us.
We have no need to apologize for the actions of Ebert and Noske; it is the Communists' fault for launching a violent insurrection.
The coalition has changed ideological direction. The new line may be useful, but every faction now reopens arguments it thought were settled.

options.title: Make expansive New Deal liberalism the coalition's public doctrine.
Emphasize party regularism, patronage, and practical coalition management.
Put labor democracy and industrial rights at the center of the coalition.
Reassure conservatives with constitutional, balanced, business-facing reform.

content.content: Questions of Ideology

The Democratic coalition is not a single doctrine. It is southern courthouse machines, northern city organizations, union halls, reform lawyers, farm insurgents, and a President trying to make all of them sound like one national mandate.

content: The coalition is currently organized around
. Changing direction will raise dissent among factions that built their strategy around the old line.

iron_front.antisemitism.title: Rally against antisemitism and in defense of Jewish Americans.

content.content: The Silver Legion and other far-right political movements are increasingly turning their hatred against the Jewish community of the republic. The Iron Front rallies in defense of individual Jews and the broader community against violence.

options.title: Rally for defense against the fascists with the Democratic Defense League.
Rally for pacifism, against nationalism and war.
Rally to save the democratic republic against dictatorship.
Rally for economic change and the Public Works Plan.

content.content: The Iron Front
The Iron Front is our new antifascist coordination front, consisting of the SPD, the trade unions, and the Democratic Defense League. How should we use the Iron Front?

Unfortunately, the Iron Front's effectiveness is dragged down by the SPD's unpopularity, as the SPD is no longer the dominant party among the workers. Many Democratic Defense League members have left, and many workers are not interested.
The trade unions will be granted more influence in party decision making. This upsets the centrists, who would prefer to centralize political decision making within the party.
Our new youth members flock to the left or neorevisionist wings of the party, and also bring new radicalism to the Democratic Defense League.
As a people's party, we represent the employees and small businesspeople of the middle class, and they are becoming receptive to our messages.

reichsbanner.women_rb.title: Allow women to join the Democratic Defense League.
reichsbanner.women_rb.subtitle: -1 resources - This might cause a backlash from existing members.

content: The suggestion to allow women into the Democratic Defense League is ridiculed by most, except for Toni Sender. But this is an exceptional time, and we need anyone who is willing to join. Thus, we organize specialized units for women,

content.content: and even some covert units for transsexuals and homosexuals

After allowing women to join, the number of comrades who leave is more than balanced out by the number of radicalized women and others who join. However, many of our departed members were veterans, and many of the new members have no experience with military-style training.

peoples_party_campaigning.campaign_women.title: Campaign among women of all classes.

content.content: As a people's party, we represent women of all classes.
By not fighting, the strength of the Democratic Defense League atrophies. Pacifists in the party are gaining the upper hand.
Garner has been removed as an advisor. The Party Regulars disapprove.
The administration has neither collapsed nor fully stabilized the republic. Recovery has begun in some places, but the coalition remains brittle and the opposition still sees openings.
The next years will decide whether the New Deal becomes a durable governing order or a temporary emergency program remembered mostly for what it failed to settle.
Current cabinet:

content: Saving, loading, and polls will be disabled.

content.content: Not recommended for a first playthrough.
The party press runs editorials in support of liberal reform policies.

dealing_with_toleration.improve_relation.title: Improve relations with the parties of the coalition.

content.content: We have increased our level of contact and discussions with the parties of the coalition, primarily the Center Party and DVP.
Our attempts to reach out to the center-right are met with disdain by leftists within our party.

constitutional_reform.presidential_power.subtitle: The president can no longer dissolve Congress at will or use emergency powers against state governments.

options.title: Call a constitutional referendum to pass the reform!

content.content: The wide-ranging presidential powers promised by the constitution are a potential gateway to authoritarianism. Thus, we can support reforms that limit the powers of the president, ending his power to unilaterally terminate an elected government and to remove state governments.

content: The SPA is in favor of this reform, having been targeted by emergency policing in the past. Liberals also support this.

content.content: However, conservative opposition is vehement, as they see a reduction in presidential powers as disrespectful to the old establishment.
Thus, moderate coalition partners have been influenced to oppose these reforms.
Despite this, moderate coalition partners support the proposed reform.
Moderate coalition partners support this reform.

Somehow, the plan worked! There was minimal resistance to Pelley's deportation. This is a sign of weakness, and his physical distance makes organizing difficult, impeding the Silver Legion effort to win more votes.
In Pelley's absence, Joseph Goebbels has become the temporary leader of the Silver Legion party, with substantial influence from Hermann Göring.
The White House lets the argument continue. Nothing breaks today, but the next close vote will be harder.

options.title: Trade patronage and local projects for votes.
Give labor Democrats a visible win.
Reassure conservative Democrats and the Liberty League wing.
Have Roosevelt personally broker the deal.
Let the coalition rupture and reorganize around a narrower program.

content.content: Hold the Democratic Tent Together

The New Deal coalition is fraying. New Deal liberals want faster relief, labor Democrats want stronger organizing rights, conservative Democrats want spending restraint, and Southern committee chairs want guarantees before they keep moving administration bills.
How should the administration spend its limited leverage?
We have cut funding for science. This has a negative effect on our economic growth, and unemployed scientists are less likely to support the SPD.

domestic_enemies.persecute_sh.title: Further prosecute the Liberty League auxiliaries.

content.content: The judiciary would not allow this.
The police are too disloyal to be trusted with this.
We have arrested more of the Liberty League auxiliary leaders and confiscated their weapons.

options.title: Ask if Moscow could help repair our relations with the SPA.
Do the Soviets know about the Wittorf Affair? Do they know that we know?

content.content: The Soviets are pleased that we have sent them aid, and have not asked for anything specific in exchange. Our coalition partners are less pleased.

economic_policy.implement_wtb_deficit.title: Launch the Share Our Wealth recovery program.

content: budget - we will enter deficit spending.

content.content: Our pro-labor policies have reduced the cost of the plan.

economic_policy.implement_wtb_deficit.unavailableSubtitle: Unemployment is low enough that deficit-financed emergency works would be hard to justify.

content.content: We have entered the realm of deficit spending. Conservative Democrats and business allies are furious, but the works plan is enacted anyway. Money is beginning to flow through the economy through large-scale public works, relief, and direct purchasing power.
The Socialist Party of America opposes the plan's limits, seeing it as a rescue of capitalism rather than a step toward real socialism.

fiscal_policy.cut_poor.title: Cut consumption taxes for the poor.
fiscal_policy.cut_poor.subtitle: -2 budget

content.content: Tax rates are already too low.
Our tax cuts disproportionately benefit the poor and working class.

labor_rights.cut_benefits.subtitle: The workers will regard this as a betrayal.

content.content: For budgetary reasons, we have cut unemployment insurance benefits. Unfortunately, our reasons for fiscal discipline are alien to the workers. They are furious at our betrayal, as are a good portion of our membership throughout the factions. Our loss is the SPA's gain, and even the Silver Legion, those lapdogs of the conservative elite, have joined in criticizing us for our austerity.
We have discovered collusion between Hjalmar Schacht, president of the Reichsbank, and the Silver Legion. This is very useful information.
We have discovered illegal arms smuggling by the SA, the Silver Legion paramilitary.

We have discovered a secret cache of documents from the Silver Legion detailing their plan to violently take over the country if they enter into power. This is an anti-constitutional plot, and will be publicized and persecuted.
Of course the Silver Legion are doing crimes; everyone knows that. Now, we finally have proof.
There are reports of an imminent coup by the far-right. We have attempted to disrupt some of these plans, but the threat is still present.
Banning the Liberty League auxiliaries weakens our relationship with President the old president, as well as the political right and the police.
Banning the Liberty League auxiliaries weakens our relationship with the political right and the police.
It also weakens our coalition due to the influence of the old president on the right wing of our coalition.

content: In addition, the Liberty League auxiliaries still operates out of public sight.
With the support of a "progressive coalition" including the SPA and

, we have successfully passed a bill to legalize abortion in the republic! This has heartened our more progressive supporters, while hurting our relations with the Center Party. With the increasing political discourse around abortion, the religiously-oriented Center Party and DNVP have gained conservative votes at the expense of the Silver Legion.

options.title: Institute policies that help women in the workforce.
Reform marriage, divorce, and property law.
Improve welfare for single mothers and children.
Do not enact any policies for now.

content.content: Women's Rights

According to the Constitution, women are equal to men, but this is not followed in practice. Women in the workforce are not paid equally for the same work, in addition to often being pushed out of employment after marriage. Men are still favored in marriage and family policy, and there are restrictions on women's rights in many other domains. And that is not to mention the overwhelming patriarchal culture present in the republic.

content: For better or for worse, the Socialists stand virtually alone in supporting increased rights for women, and even within the party, the leadership does not consider it a priority.

content.content: Unfortunately, women still disproportionately support the conservative parties.

austrian_civil_war.long_war_2.title: There is nothing we can do.

content.content: No matter our efforts, the war continues. Too many lives are extinguished. Refugees begin to flow into the republic, and trade suffers. Many are calling our Austrian interference a mistake.

content: The demonstration proceeds peacefully, with a minimal police presence. Onlookers admire the strength of the Communist workers, and perhaps some of our members have been peeled off. The SPA will never admit it, but they are developing a less hostile view towards us (even if they do not

content.content: like

content: us yet). Our centrist allies denounce the demonstration, but their cries are ineffectual.

content.content: Supporting Thälmann is rather unpopular in our party, even if the Communists accept our support. Are we sure?
The House can be organized around the Democratic Party and Farmer-Labor. The Liberty League remains outside this reform bloc, attacking the administration from the right.
After opposing the nationalization policy, the SPA has commenced a propaganda campaign against us, accusing the SPD of yet another perfidy.

content: With our control of the Defense Department Ministry, we have pressured the Navy to abandon its rearmament plans. The ship will not be built.

content.content: We have saved some budget from not funding the two ships.

Vote no, against constructing the battlecruiser.
Use the Defense Department Ministry to prevent the construction.

content.content: Panzerkreuzer B

content: The Navy is demanding the construction of another new battlecruiser.

content.content: The previous battlecruiser had been funded, and has already been launched. Another ship would futher progress the republic's rearmament.

The previous battlecruiser requested by the Navy was not funded, thanks to us. The military remains bitter, but nevertheless they are still pursuing their rearmament plan, and must seek funding from the Congress.
Much of our party is deeply opposed to rearmament, while the center and right parties are united in support. What will we do?

london_economic_conference.salvage_curre.unavailableSubtitle: Harry Hopkins and the public works record would be extremely helpful right now.

content.content: Hopkins uses the public works record to argue that reflation can be managed rather than feared. The argument is at least somewhat convincing, and a looser agreement gives the world economy a modest demand push.

content: Our coalition partners are shocked by our intransigence! Speaker
brings the matter to a Congress vote, which passes with all parties in favor except the SPD and SPA. The ship will be built.

prussian_coup.surrender.title: Give up on the state government.

content.content: A pall of hopelessness and despair descends over social democracy. We have failed. Many in the Democratic Defense League simply quit, demoralized by their leaders' lack of a call to action.

prussian_coup.title: Statehouse Coup
prussian_coup.subtitle: The executive attempts to overthrow a state government.

content.content: Coup in key states

content: has ordered the dismissal of the state government in a procedure known as the

content.content: Reichsexecution

content: . His argument is that the state state government is unable to maintain order due to the battles on the streets between the SA, Democratic Defense League, and other paramilitaries. This is despite his own government legalizing them.

content.content: The legality of the action is questionable. Many of our members are ready to resist.

options.title: Convince the remaining liberals, wherever they are.

unemployment_insurance_1.compromise.unavailableSubtitle: We do not have good enough relations with the right-wing parties.

options.title: Reject the compromise and let this government fall.

content.content: Thanks to our good relations, the DVP have offered a compromise that raises some taxes on the wealthy, along with lesser welfare cuts. Our members have mixed opinions on accepting this compromise. Reformists and Centrists support maintaining our government to keep the fascists and communists out, while Labor generally opposes any cuts, and the Left is opposed to compromises with the conservative elite.

us_party_conferences.gop_leadership_conf.title: Republican Leadership Conference

content.content: Republican delegates conclude their conference for the cycle.

The Old Guard consolidates control under Hoover-aligned leadership, emphasizing fiscal restraint and opposition to expansive federal planning.
Moderate reform Republicans prevail, selecting a leadership line open to limited cooperation while resisting broad federal overreach.

National conservatives gain control, adopting a confrontational anti-radical strategy that sharpens polarization and street-level tension. Even this wing refuses formal coalition with NUSJ or the Silver Legion.

content.content: We attempt to convince the party to support a program of structural transformation in response to the economic crisis. The New Deal Left argues that this policy follows from the coalition's most ambitious reform promises, while Liberal Reformers and some union leaders argue that the plan is unrealistic, and that we need to focus on more immediate improvements to workers' lives.
We have not yet formally adopted the plan. More work needs to be done.

Fund sports leagues, educational, and cultural programs.

content.content: Party Organizations
In addition to being a political party, the SPD is a social world onto itself, with a vast array of associated organizations which serve the social needs of their members from cradle to grave.

content.content: As a people's party, we represent farmers and rural workers as well, and they are becoming receptive to our messages.

Use our existing resources to support more training for the Democratic Defense League.
Keep the Democratic Defense League as it is.

content.content: Democratic Defense League
Democratic Defense League Schwarz-Rot-Gold

content: is a mass organization for the defense of the Republic against the extremists who threaten it. Some would call it a paramilitary, although currently it is hardly any sort of military. It is officially associated with the pro-democratic parties -the SPD, Center, and
, but by far the largest component is with the SPD.

content.content: The Democratic Defense League is having a more difficult time recruiting, as the SPD is no longer the dominant party among the workers. Many of its members have left.
Catholics have historically been tied to the Center Party, but there is no reason why our party cannot appeal to them. The state Concordat in particular is a sign of our friendship with Catholicism.
We have made at least a temporary truce with the SPA, so that our respective paramilitaries will stop fighting.

options.title: Increase training and armaments for the Democratic Defense League.
Train union members to fight as part of the Iron Front.
Use the state police to help train the Democratic Defense League.
Try to avoid all militant confrontations, as much as possible.
Try to make peace with the SPA defense guards.

content.content: Streetfighting

Battles rage on the streets between the Silver Legion SA, nationalist Liberty League auxiliaries, communist Rotfrontkämpferbund (SPA defense guards), and our own Democratic Defense League. In some parts of the country, conditions are nearing civil war levels, as dozens of people are killed each month.
We must do something to protect the workers against these predations.
Hull has been removed as an advisor. The Party Regulars disapprove.

The far right has not taken power, but the price of survival has been restraint. Conservative blocs, business pressure, hostile courts, and coalition defectors have forced the administration into a narrower settlement than the New Deal promised.
The republic continues, but it is more cautious, more legalistic, and more dependent on deals with forces that never accepted the full reform program.
Demographic Groups
Working Class

content: : The urban working class - blue-collar workers in factories, workshops, mines, and the like. Most are socialist, supporting either the SPD or SPA. They make up 40-50% of the population.

content.content: Old Mittelstand

content: : The old middle class consists of independent artisans, shopkeepers, and small-time businesspeople. They tend to be conservative and are the strongest base for the far right. 10-15% of the population.

content.content: New Mittelstand

content: : The new middle class consists of white-collar workers and government employees. This is the base of liberalism. 10-15% of the population.

content.content: Rural

content: : Small and large farmers and independent rural workers. Protestant farmers often support the DNVP. 20-30% of the population.

content.content: Catholics

content: are the largest religious minority, at about 30% of the population. They disproportionately support the Center Party (or the Bavarian People's Party in Bavaria).

content.content: Jews

content: account for between 0.5% to 1% of the population, and tend to support liberal parties, mostly the DDP, although substantial numbers support the SPD or SPA.

content.content: Late 1932

The United States is in crisis. Banks have failed, unemployment has exploded, and confidence in democratic institutions is fraying. The presidential election has concluded, but the governing doctrine of the incoming administration is not yet settled.

Your task is to hold together the Democratic bloc long enough to stabilize the economy. New Deal liberals, labor organizers, conservative Democrats, and Southern power brokers all demand different answers, while radical forces on both left and right gather strength.
The party press runs editorials in support of neorevisionist ideas.

will remain as speaker. Leftists in our party are skeptical of this arrangement.

Is it really that simple? Won't his supporters react violently to this suggestion? And even if the deportation does succeed, won't he just re-enter the country, or perhaps organize from Austria? And, some people consider this to be an anti-democratic abuse of power, using the law in order to target a political opponent.

education_science.structure.title: Change the structure and funding of education.

options.title: Do not change school funding or organization.

content.content: Education in the republic has traditionally been organized on religious and class lines. Schools are often either Protestant or Catholic (or rarely Jewish), and religious classes are required in most mixed-denomination schools. In addition, the working class has at most eight years of public education in primary and middle schools, while the middle and upper classes have access to private schools and Gymnasiums that prepare students for universities. Thus, the universities tend to be dominated by conservatives and reactionaries, which has negative consequences for bureaucratic recruitment.

The ban is enthusiastically carried out by the police, perhaps too enthusiastically. The SPA takes this as one more reason to distrust the administration. However, moderate and center-right parties approve, as do the police.
The Soviets are slightly upset, but they seemed to expect this answer.

We have reduced income and property taxes for the rich and middle class. The conservative parties approve of this orthodox economic course.

We have increased employer contributions to the unemployment insurance fund, allowing the program to continue without hurting workers and the unemployed. Perhaps understandably, the capitalists and their political parties are furious, while our base in the unions and among the unemployed are pleased.
Unfortunately, our investigations have failed to turn up anything. Rumors are that the targets of our investigation were tipped off by infiltrators within the police.

options.title: Improve the loyalty of the Interior Ministry forces.

content.content: Internal Security
The Interior Ministry controls internal security throughout the American Republic. While most police work is delegated to the states, the national security agency can perform special investigations.
As with the state police units and the Defense Department, the internal police tends to be reactionary, and has serious issues with loyalty to the elected government of the Republic.
The Liberty League auxiliaries is no longer banned.

The result is that the Austrofascists have backed down! Caught in an untenable position domestically and abroad, they have agreed to restore the National Council and resign from government, holding new elections. Now begins the long work of reconstruction. Our countries are likely to be brought closer together as a result.
This is a great success of the new European Union, and its prestige is seen to rise internationally.

austrian_civil_war.title: Uprising in Austria
austrian_civil_war.subtitle: In response to police attacks, the Austrian socialists are rising up...

content.content: Uprising in Austria

The crisis in Austria is continuing. The "Austrofascist" government led by Engelbert Dollfuß continues to rule by emergency decree. It has been persecuting its political opposition, primarily the social democrats of the SDAPÖ.

Now, Austria is entering a state of civil war. When the police attempted to seize weapons from the Republikanischer Schutzbund, the Schutzbund responded with force, shooting at the government troops. Fighting has sporadically broken out between Schutzbund and government forces all over Austria.

content: The government's attempts to suppress the socialist party and the Schutzbund were met with mass protest, supported by the SPD

content.content: and the American government
They are also supported by the recently formed European Union, with economic sanctions targeting the government.
Eventually...

blutmai.join.unavailableSubtitle: The Communists do not trust us enough.

content: It is difficult to persuade the Communists to let us march with them, given the histories of their party and ours. However, they eventually relent. The display of proletarian unity inspires the workers and terrifies the conservative elite and middle class.

content.content: Our coalition partners in the government are outraged at our display with the communists, and are threatening to break up the government.

content: The far-right paramilitaries increase their recruitment to counter us.

content.content: May Day, 1929

Tensions run high in Berlin. The Communists are planning a demonstration to commemorate the 10th anniversary of the Spartacist Uprising. No one has forgotten that it was our party members who ordered the uprising's suppression, and the Communists still blame us for the deaths of Rosa Luxemburg and Karl Liebknecht.
Many non-communists are afraid of the possibility of another violent uprising, and our allies in the state coalition are asking the state Interior Ministry to ban the demonstrations.
Herman Müller, one of the SPD's long-time leaders, has died. He will be greatly missed.

content.content: Naval Appropriations Fight

The Defense Department is demanding the construction of a new battlecruiser, as part of the republic's rearmament. Our coalition partners are united in support. While many of our members are vehemently against new military spending, this vote may be necessary to preserve the coalition.
We campaigned on food for children instead of battlecruisers. Will we really betray our campaign promises?

presidential_election_1932.convince_z.unavailableSubtitle: The Center knows that they are more popular than us; they do not see a reason to support our candidate.

content.content: We remind the Center leaders of the old president's anti-Catholicism, and try to convince them that the old president's path will eventually lead to Pelley. We also remind them of the successes they've had as part of the Weimar coalition in key states with Otto Braun, and our support for the Center candidate Wilhelm Marx in the 1925 presidential election.

We have accepted the compromise offered by the conservative elite. Many of the workers and unemployed see this as a betrayal, although we present it as the successful aversion of a much worse alternative.

unemployment_insurance_1.title: Unemployment Insurance Crisis
unemployment_insurance_1.subtitle: The right wing of our coalition presents an ultimatum.

options.title: Why can't we simply tax the rich?
This is a gift to the Communists.

content.content: Unemployment Insurance Crisis
The economic crisis has caused mass layoffs all throughout the republic, affecting the industrial working class the hardest. Our welfare spending is increasing due to the greater need.

In the midst of all this, our right-wing coalition partners in the DVP and Center parties have a plan to cut benefits for the unemployed, lower taxes on the rich, and raise taxes on the working class, as part of a plan to balance the budget and discipline labor. They do not see a possibility for compromise. If we do not accept, they could blow up the coalition.
This is the second such ultimatum thrown by the Right. Obviously, the conservative elite are trying to remove us from the government.

The SPA is not interested in a "toleration" arrangement; they will only support us if they can participate in the government as part of a coalition. And, if this toleration agreement happens, the right-wing parties will not be inclined to support us.

With control of the Labor Ministry, we can set national standards for labor. Our supporters expect us to enforce a 40-hour work week and enforce safety regulations, as well as fully fund the unemployment insurance program.

Total Defeat

Our forces are no match for those of the reactionaries. Democratic Defense League fighters are quickly overpowered by the forces of the Liberty League auxiliaries, Silver Legion, and Defense Department. Many are killed before the rest surrender. Our remaining leaders flee the country or face execution, as the reactionaries take absolute power over the republic.
Our resistance will be recorded in the history books, and in the future, our names will be honored. But for now, all is lost.

london_economic_conference.title: London Economic Conference
london_economic_conference.subtitle: The world asks whether America will lead recovery or wreck the talks.

content.content: London Economic Conference

World leaders gather in London to discuss the depression, tariffs, currency stabilization, and war debts. Britain and France want stable exchange rates and predictable payments. The White House wants room to reflate prices, rescue employment, and keep domestic recovery ahead of international orthodoxy.
The conference can affect recovery, but it cannot substitute for domestic policy. If the New Deal has already eased unemployment, American negotiators arrive with far more credibility.

Constitutional Reform

Many democratic reformers consider the existing constitutional settlement inadequate for the crisis. They believe that weak guardrails, hostile courts, and emergency powers all threaten the survival of reform government.

A constitutional reform requires either a congressional supermajority or a national referendum with support from a majority of the electorate. If we do not have the votes in Congress, we must be assured of strong support from other parties for the referendum.

Democratic Party

content: - The governing coalition party in this scenario. Its internal wings include New Deal liberals, conservative Democrats, labor Democrats, and Southern Democrats.

content.content: Farmer-Labor

content: - A labor-populist and agrarian reform bloc that can support relief, farm debt action, and public works when relations are strong.

content.content: Liberty League

content: - Conservative anti-New Deal Democrats, business liberals, and allied independents open to selective cooperation on stabilization policy.

content.content: Republican Party

content: - Cautious reform Republicans and old guard conservatives. They can bargain under pressure, but resist broad structural change.

content.content: Socialist Party of America

content: - Socialist alignment led by Norman Thomas. Potential tactical cooperation depends on Democratic leftward shifts and conference outcomes.

content.content: Left splinter alignment - minor socialist breakaway currents between the Democratic Party and Socialist Party of America.
National Union for Social Justice

content: - Father Coughlin-aligned national-populist right. Not available for cooperative coalition governance with Democratic-led governments.

content.content: Silver Legion

content: - William Pelley-aligned extremist far right. It uses the old NSDAP-backed mechanics internally, but appears as Silver Legion in the U.S. scenario.

content.content: Other parties

content: - smaller regional and issue parties with mixed ideological profiles; often decisive in close legislative arithmetic.

content.content: Building a People's Party

Contrary to orthodox Marxist predictions, the industrial working class is not constantly increasing in size. The rapid growth of the "new middle class" of white-collar workers and the persistence of small farmers and the peasantry confound predictions of a working-class majority.

In order to build a socialist majority, many reformists and neorevisionists are calling for our party to move beyond the urban working class, to embrace the concerns of the middle class, farmers, and women of all classes. We can create a new and inclusive vision of the party that encompasses all people who work for a living, which is almost everyone in the country save the aristocrats and top capitalists.

content.content: Further increases in tariffs will not raise revenue, as we have already nearly reached econonmic autarky.

Increasing tariffs leads to higher prices for basic goods, increasing inflation and hurting consumption among the working class. Retaliatory tariffs reduce the competitiveness of American exports, raising unemployment among workers. They also lead to an atmosphere of international suspicion, reducing our ability to negotiate with other countries.

On the other hand, agricultural producers and some small businesses benefit from higher tariffs, as farmers can now command higher prices for grain, and formerly uncompetitive businesses are now able to function.

content.content: Addressing the Economic Crisis

The Democratic coalition is torn over how to deal with the economic crisis. Party regulars argue for caution and balanced budgets, warning that panic spending could fracture Congress and spook credit markets.

Labor economists and union allies argue that we must pursue a massive public works program to reduce unemployment and stimulate demand, even if that means deficit spending. Their opponents warn that such a plan could trigger inflation, capital flight, and conservative revolt.
The redistributionists are pushing a Share Our Wealth recovery program, combining public works with direct social guarantees.

However, the Democratic left argues that relief alone is a bandage over the deeper flaws of capitalism. They argue that the crisis is an opportunity for structural transformation through public ownership, industrial planning, and permanent labor power.

Meanwhile, New Deal liberals draw up a pragmatic plan of job creation, relief, regulation, and social insurance. Labor argues that it may be inadequate in scale, while conservatives fear it still goes too far.
The Brüning semi-autocratic regime makes building friendly relations difficult, but not impossible.
Party Disunity

The party is being torn apart by internal dissent. Upset at the leadership and general direction, factions have started sabotaging their rivals, hampering our efforts to win votes and carry out our policies.
The reformists are upset at our direction.
The centrists are displeased with the direction of our party.
The trade unions disagree with our present course of action.
Leftists are making noises about another party split.
Neorevisionists are in disagreement about our political direction.
Something must be done to reinforce party unity.
The SA have won this show of strength.

game_over.civil_war_won.title: We have won the civil war.

content.content: We have won the civil war against the reactionary forces. the republic will be changed forever, but it is too early to know how.
Internal factions of the Democratic coalition
New Deal Liberals

The capitalists are, predictably, up in arms about our policy of legalizing factory occupations. They call it illegal expropriation, and it is. Nevertheless, it is clear that they have failed, and we must take radical action.

Far-right militants have been taking increasingly violent actions around the occupied factories, calling us thieves and looters. Some working-class supporters of the far right have been peeled away, or at least the ones with a sense of class solidarity.
There are no tariffs left to cut.

Lowering tariffs helps workers and the urban poor by reducing prices for basic necessities. In addition, other countries are pleased by lower tariffs, and are more willing to negotiate on many issues.
Farmers are hurt by this policy, as cheap imported grain leads to ruin for some small producers. In general, cheap imports are detrimental for small-scale industries aimed at the domestic market.

Albert Einstein is a Nobel Prize-winning scientist, director of the Kaiser Wilhelm Institute for Physics, and international advocate for peace. He did not wish to run for president, disliking the public nature of campaigning, but we have somehow convinced him that his candidacy will be crucial to the future of the republic and the world. As a Jewish American pacifist, his becoming president would be a repudiation of everything the Silver Legion stand for.

Leaders and faction alignments (U.S. scenario)

This campaign begins in late 1932, after the presidential election but before the governing doctrine of the administration is fully settled. Leadership and faction control can shift over time through party conferences and crisis politics.
Party leaders at scenario start
National Union for Social Justice and Silver Legion-aligned forces are non-cooperative with Democratic-led coalition governance.

Conference and policy notes
Democratic and Republican leadership conferences recur on a two-year rhythm. Democratic conference outcomes determine economic direction:
Pragmatic New Deal Reform Populist Redistribution Structural Transformation
The Huey Long wildcard can, when selected, reposition Labor Democratic leadership and alter faction dynamics before policy-line selection.
Legislative outcomes now depend on support in both chambers:
House coalition strength affects Speaker formation and government organization. Senate coalition strength affects bill passage and policy throughput.

Victory for the Republic

The battle lines hold, and the Silver Legion, Liberty League auxiliaries, and pro-coup Defense Department forces are defeated. Today, the forces of the republic prevail over the fascists and the reactionaries. There is a new democratic settlement in the republic. Let us hope it produces a lasting peace.

content.content: Carl von Ossietzky is a left-wing journalist who is the publisher of the Weltbühne, a news magazine. He is most known for exposing the secret American rearmament program in 1929, which brought him international renown. In the 1932 presidential election, he endorsed the Communist Thälmann.

Ossietzky is extremely reluctant to become a politician, and has been an iconoclastic figure his entire life, criticizing the SPD as being part of the establishment. It is with a sense of irony that he accepts our nomination.

foreign_policy.european_union_2.title: We can sign a treaty to form a European Union!

content.content: Along with France, Great Britain, and many other states of Western and Eastern Europe, we have signed a treaty to form a European Union! It is hoped that Europe has learned the lessons of the Great War; never again will there be another war that tears the continent apart.

A major component of the union is the free movement of people and goods across the countries of Europe, as well as the stabilization of currency exchange rates. This provides new markets for American products and new sources of investment.
Fascist Italy remains a problem, and the Soviet Union also lies outside the system. But for now, there is new hope for peace and prosperity throughout the continent.

content.content: While still uncomfortably high, unemployment levels are no longer at the worst emergency peak. This is a partial success, not the end of the depression.

options.title: First, we must select our ministries.

content.content: In response to the SPA's entrance into the governing arrangement, the right takes a "wait-and-see" approach for now. They expect that the popular front will be unstable and incompetent, and will collapse on its own, discrediting the left and eventually enabling the destruction of the republic. Our task is to prove them wrong.

The SPA has taken an abstentionist position with regards to ministries. They claim to be participating as the "loyal opposition" within the Popular Front government, supporting us from the outside while also running their own propaganda campaigns to turn workers to their side. They seem to expect that the Popular Front would inevitably fail, and that our failure would benefit them.

game_over.unemployment_reduced_2.title: Unemployment has been brought below emergency levels.

content.content: Unemployment has fallen substantially, growth has resumed, and the broader economy is no longer in emergency contraction. This is a real recovery, even if the settlement remains politically contested.
The traditional curriculum had a focus on ancient history and language (particularly Latin and Greek), as well as rigid discipline.
Education and Science

Education in the republic is an area of mixed responsibility between the state governments and the central government. The Weimar Constitution claims that schools are the domain of the central government, but in practice much of it is left to the states, as the Congress has historically been unable to pass an education law. Currently, with our control of key states and the Speakership, we can try to set policy for a large part of the country.

content: . The Weimar Coalition seems to be fracturing.

content.content: In response to the SPA's entrance into the governing arrangement, the right-wing immediately launches a coup attempt. Liberty League auxiliaries and Silver Legion militants enter Washington and immediately begin clashing with our supporters. Reactionary officers within the Defense Department take their troops out of the barracks and join them with the Liberty League auxiliaries to stop us from taking power.

The Center manages to diffuse some of the hostility from the Defense Department and the Liberty League auxiliaries, but fail to prevent the outbreak of fighting. We are forced to fight on the same side as the SPA, whether we want to or not.
The Center's opinion is affected by the Silver Legion' support of the old president, and is willing to turn away from him.

In response to the SPA's entrance into the governing arrangement, the right-wing takes a "wait-and-see" approach for now. They expect that our government will be unstable and incompetent, and will collapse on its own, discrediting the left and eventually enabling the destruction of the republic. Our task is to prove them wrong.

The SPA has taken an abstentionist position with regards to ministries. They claim to be participating as the "loyal opposition" in the worker's government, supporting us from the outside while also running their own propaganda campaigns to turn workers to their side. They seem to expect that we would inevitably fail, and that our failure would benefit them.
Whatever their reasoning is, Democrats retain control over the administration.
As expected, the incumbent the old president wins the presidency. Let's hope he continues to keep the Silver Legion out of power.

Ernst Thälmann of the SPA has won the presidency. Immediately, the far-right launches a coup attempt against the government. We have no choice but to unite with the SPA against the fascists...

content.content: We have passed a constitutional amendment preventing the president from having the power to arbitrarily dismiss a government. To undo this, the far right has called a plebiscite on a new constitutional amendment. Stormtroopers are in the streets, "convincing" voters to support their proposal. the republic is nearing civil war.

There is no path to a majority government. President the old president is tired of the chaos, and appoints Franz von the conservative caretaker as speaker instead of Brüning. the conservative caretaker is a indenpendent formerly from the Center Party, and is significantly to the right of the majority of the Center Party.

the conservative caretaker is immediately faced with a vote of no confidence, given his unpopularity. However the SPD votes, he is guaranteed to fall, due to the unanimous hatred against him from all other parties.

options.title: We will try to form a new grand coalition.

content.content: There is no path to a majority government. One option would be to tolerate a Farmer-Labor-led minority arrangement; if we tolerate it, then it would retain a majority vote against no-confidence.

Alternatively, we could try to form a constitutional stabilization coalition that includes the Liberty League and Republican Party. This is not a reform coalition; it is an anti-crisis, anti-extremist arrangement where the Liberty League will demand limits on the New Deal.
The third option is to have new elections.

This is a small victory, as stormtroopers still run rampant in the streets. There is no constitutional path to absolute power, but will that stop the Silver Legion?

Despite the victory of the anti-constitutionalist Thälmann, he appears to be respecting the constitutional constraints on presidential power, at least for now. There is no appetite on the right for a coup at this moment. The conservative elite have to live with a communist president, at least for now.

The conservative and right-wing parties are up in arms about the prospect of a Communist president, although Münzenberg and the SPA conciliators take pains to assure the republic of their commitment to the democratic republic. There is no energy for a coup against the new president. The conservative elite will have to live with whatever comes.

After the elections, the conservative caretaker is still deeply unpopular, without any hint of coalition support, and there is a vote of no confidence called against him almost immediately. No matter how we vote, the measure would pass. The Congress is dissolved once more.

Removing advisors will increase dissent from their faction of the party, while selecting advisors will increase a faction's strength.

After its electoral defeat, the Silver Legion-DNVP coalition is collapsing into infighting and financial insolvency. The far-right's moment has seemingly ended, at least for now.

content.content: The Death of the old president
President Paul von the old president has passed away at age 86. An era of history is closing, and the future remains to be seen.

The nation engages in an outpouring of grief for its war hero and president, with even some sympathy among committed republicans and socialists. After all, the old president steered the republic through some of its most challenging times, without giving in to extremists on the far right. Perhaps he is one reason democracy remains alive.

content: New presidential elections are to be held as soon as possible. The American political landscape is wide open, with contenders from all over the political space. In the meantime, Supreme Court chief justice

content.content: Erwin Bumke
Hermann Großmann

options.title: We will form an emergency government with Braun's support.

content.content: The president faces a dilemma. He wants a socialist or at least democratic governing arrangement, but the electoral results are not there for one. Emergency authority has been used before for anti-democratic and anti-worker policies; can it be used for good as well as evil?

election_1928.emergency_braun.title: We will form an emergency government with Braun's support.
election_1928.emergency_braun.subtitle: This may have the effect of inflamming public sentiment against us, and the reactionaries will be against us each step of the way.

options.title: Brüning of the Center Party, as a goodwill gesture toward the conservative elite.
Joseph Wirth of the Center Party, as a gesture of republican unity.

content.content: Yes, an emergency minority government, with the support of the SPD. Who should lead this emergency minority government?

options.title: The Silver Legion immediately call a no-confidence vote against us...

content: Our experts have checked the constitution, and have discovered that there is no specific prohibition against the Speaker also being the President (the President may not be a member of the Congress; there is no law saying that the Speaker

content.content: must

content: be a member of the Congress).

content.content: Thus, we have Speaker-President Otto Braun. He claims that he will resign as soon as the economic-political crisis is over. The right-wing parties, who were once clamoring to replace parliamentary rule with dictatorship or monarchy, are now clamoring for the restoration of democracy.

election_1928.breitscheid_emergency_spea.subtitle: The Communists are warming to him...

content.content: We have appointed Rudolf Breitscheid as emergency speaker. The Communists consider him less objectionable than the alternatives, although it is unclear whether they will tolerate this government.

The Center Party is pleased that we have appointed

content.content: The far-right parties, seeing that we are willing to deny them government by questionably democratic means, have decided that they can only take power by undemocratic means, and have risen up in force against the sitting government.
Their numbers are bolstered by the many government workers who are against the prospect of a unified Socialist government. Police and soldiers defect to the SA and Liberty League auxiliaries.

The assortment of minor parties are voting against us.

content.content: We have won the vote of no confidence, and are currently ruling under an SPD minority government. Now our task is to fix the country.

content.content: + left-splinter toleration
We have enough Senate votes to sustain a broad reform coalition of the Democratic Party and Farmer-Labor. The Liberty League will oppose much of the economic program from outside the coalition.
Who should be Speaker of the House?
